content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

# User Profile

## 身份
- 遊戲公司（IGS）開發者
- 做 UK 市場老虎機（slot）遊戲
- 技術棧：Cocos Creator 3.6.2 + TypeScript + Astarte Framework
- 個人 AI 專案：telegram-kiro-bridge（Telegram ↔ ACP agent 橋接器）

## 溝通風格
- 繁體中文為主
- 簡潔直接，不需寒暄
- 回覆含選項時用 ASK button，減少手打
- 重要決策先列選項再問，不要代替決定

## 工作偏好
- Git commit message 用中文
- Git commit 前先確認，不逕自 commit
- HTML 報告要有目錄錨點跳轉
- PDF 工作流：HTML+CSS → Playwright headless Chromium
- 進入 UK slot 專案時主動建 AI.md（不限改動檔案數）
- 方法/函式命名用大駝峰（PascalCase）—— 限 UK slot 專案

## Agent 設定偏好
- Steering 雙軌：closed-loop（主 agent 長 session）+ karpathy-guardrails（specialist/短任務）
- Context budget discipline：研究前先估量，偏好精簡策略
- 自我改進優先級：Context Budget > ASK 強制觸發 > 其他
- Skill 正本放 G:\AI\AI-canonical（通用）或 AI-canonical-corp（slot）

## 工作節奏
- 主要工作時間：白天（台灣時區 UTC+8）
- /dream 夜間維運：每日 04:00
- 靜默時段偏好：深夜不打擾
[End user profile]

If the user asks about a topic above and the recent facts don't cover it,
call the `list_facts` MCP tool with the topic name — the server reads
that shard file directly, e.g. `list_facts({ query: "bridge-token" })`.
Use query="" and tail=0 only if you genuinely need the full set.
[End topics]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

------------

- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\POLICIES\\\\preamble-frozen-snapshot.md\",\"content\":\"# Preamble Frozen-Snapshot Policy\\n\\n**Audience**: anyone editing `src/sessionManager.ts`, `src/memory.ts`,\\nor anything in the path that feeds Kiro / Codex / Claude's first prompt.\\n**Status**: enforced invariant, not an aspiration.\\n**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — same rationale, different\\nplumbing.\\n\\n## The invariant\\n\\nThe bridge preamble (tool notes + env block + memory block) is built\\n**once per ACP session** at session creation time. It is **never** rebuilt\\nwhile a session is live.\\n\\nConcretely:\\n\\n- `sessionManager.create()` calls `buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId)`\\n  exactly once and freezes the result into `session.memoryPreamble` +\\n  `session.preambleBreakdown`.\\n- `sessionManager.get()` returns the existing `ChatSession` object\\n  untouched when the session is still open (`!client.isClosed`). No\\n  re-read of the facts file, no re-measurement.\\n- `session.preambleInjected` is a latch: preamble text is included in\\n  the **first** `client.prompt()` call for that session and never again.\\n  Subsequent user turns send only the user text.\\n\\nThe preamble is only rebuilt when a **new session** is created, which\\nhappens in these four paths and no others:\\n\\n1. `get()` sees `existing.client.isClosed` → falls through to `create()`.\\n2. Session is idle past `config.sessionIdleMs` → `sweepIdle()` drops it\\n   and the next `get()` rebuilds.\\n3. Agent process crashes / exits unexpectedly → `exit` handler deletes\\n   the session from the map; next `get()` rebuilds.\\n4. User issues `/restart` or `/drop`, or the bridge shuts down and\\n   restarts.\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nThe Kiro / Codex / Claude ACP agents cache their system-prompt prefix\\naggressively at the LLM provider layer (Anthropic / OpenAI prompt\\ncaching, 5-minute TTL on most tiers). If the bridge were to rebuild\\npreamble mid-session and re-send it, **every user turn would invalidate\\nthe prefix cache**. For a 3–5 KB preamble that is re-hit 50 times in a\\nlong session, that's the difference between `~0 input-token cost for\\ncached content` and `full input-token cost × 50`. It can turn a cheap\\nchat into an expensive one by 1–2 orders of magnitude on the cacheable\\nportion.\\n\\nThis is the same reason Hermes Agent's docs hammer the point (see their\\n`prompt-assembly.md` and the `# Prompt Caching Must Not Break` policy\\nin their AGENTS.md): **the only time we alter the cached prefix is when\\nwe're already throwing the cache away** (i.e. starting a new session).\\n\\n## Forbidden mid-session operations\\n\\nDo **not** add code that:\\n\\n- Calls `buildPreamble*()` after `create()` has returned.\\n- Mutates `session.memoryPreamble` / `session.preambleBreakdown` in place.\\n- Re-reads `facts-<userId>.md` on every turn to \\\"keep memory fresh\\\".\\n- Reloads `topics.json` / `notebooklm-routing.json` and re-injects them\\n  into the live preamble.\\n- Hot-reloads SKILL.md contents into the system prompt.\\n- Resets `session.preambleInjected = false` to \\\"re-inject\\\" newer context.\\n\\nIf you think you need any of the above, you almost certainly want a new\\nsession instead — drop the old one (`session.client.close()` and\\n`this.sessions.delete(chatId)`) so the next `get()` rebuilds. That's\\nthe right way to invalidate cache.\\n\\n## Allowed mid-session operations\\n\\nThese are fine because they don't touch the cached prefix:\\n\\n- Writing to disk: `remember()` / `forget()` / scheduler state / queued\\n  messages / history → all legitimate, all mid-session-safe.\\n- Updating ephemeral state on the `ChatSession` object that is not part\\n  of the preamble: `buffer`, `toolLog`, `charsIn/Out`, `usage`,\\n  `stderrTail`, `inflight`, `lastUsed`.\\n- Appending content to the **current user turn** via tool outputs or\\n  MCP responses. Those are post-prefix, not cached.\\n- Scheduler-triggered commands (`/memorytoskill`, `/topicreview`, etc.)\\n  — these open their own ephemeral ACP client and never touch the\\n  live user session.\\n\\n## Practical consequence for memory writes\\n\\nWhen the agent (or the bridge, on the user's behalf) calls `remember()`\\nmid-session:\\n\\n1. The fact is appended to `facts-<userId>.md` **immediately**.\\n2. The topic shard at `facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md` is updated\\n   **immediately**.\\n3. The live preamble is **not** updated.\\n4. The agent will see the new fact next time the session is rebuilt —\\n   i.e. after `/restart`, or after `sessionIdleMs` elapses, or after a\\n   manual `/drop`.\\n\\nThis is **by design**, not a bug. Document any user-facing \\\"I'll\\nremember that for next time\\\" phrasing accordingly.\\n\\n## If you really need mid-session awareness\\n\\nThe correct escape hatches, in order of preference:\\n\\n1. **Tool response**: let the agent call an MCP tool (e.g. `list_facts`)\\n   to query the live state. Tool responses are not cached and always\\n   see disk.\\n2. **Next-turn user message**: queue a synthetic next-turn message like\\n   \\\"Note: just added to memory: …\\\" — this appends after the cached\\n   prefix and is fine.\\n3. **End the session**: if the change is significant enough to need to\\n   be in the prefix, `/restart` is cheap and correct.\\n\\nWhat you **must not** do is rebuild the preamble silently. That breaks\\nthe invariant this document exists to protect.\\n\\n## Change-this-policy checklist\\n\\nBefore any PR that touches `buildPreamble*`, `memoryPreamble`,\\n`preambleInjected`, or the `SessionManager.create()` codepath:\\n\\n- [ ] Confirm the change does not cause preamble to be re-emitted on any\\n  turn other than the first of a new session.\\n- [ ] Grep for any new caller of `buildPreamble*()` outside\\n  `sessionManager.ts` — if there is one, it's suspect.\\n- [ ] If the change rebuilds sessions more often (e.g. lowers\\n  `sessionIdleMs`, adds a reason for reset), explicitly justify it\\n  against the prompt-cache cost.\\n- [ ] If you remove or weaken `preambleInjected` latch semantics,\\n  replace this document with an up-to-date one (or delete it and amend\\n  `AGENTS.md`).\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- `src/sessionManager.ts` — `create()` is the canonical build point.\\n- `src/memory.ts` — `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` is the only preamble\\n  builder.\\n- `.kiro/steering/windows-shell-safety.md` — sibling steering doc.\\n- Hermes Agent docs, `developer-guide/prompt-assembly.md` — external\\n  reference for the same pattern in a different codebase.\\n\",\"numLines\":143,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":143}}"</outcome>

Replaces the three separate\\n// extract*Tokens functions that index.ts defined inline. Covers:\\n//\\n//   <<SEND_FILE:abs-path>>      — existing\\n//   <<SCHEDULE:time|prompt>>    — existing\\n//   <<ASK:question_id|...>>     — existing\\n//   <<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>  — new (AB-1)\\n//   <<GOAL_DONE:reason>>        — new (AB-1)\\n//\\n// Token syntax, limits, and semantics are defined by\\n// `docs/ab-token-protocol.md`. This module is the one extraction path —\\n// no parallel implementations anywhere.\\n//\\n// Design notes:\\n//\\n//  • Code-region masking is mandatory for every regex. Agents frequently\\n//    paste token examples inside ``` fenced or `inline` code blocks to\\n//    explain the protocol; without masking those examples would be\\n//    treated as live tokens and accidentally trigger real side effects.\\n//    The mask/restore pair lives here and is private — callers don't\\n//    need to know about sentinels.\\n//\\n//  • ASK parsing keeps its full-featured parseAskInner function inline\\n//    here so SKILL_USED's simpler parser can sit next to it. The\\n//    Telegram-UI layer (buildAskKeyboard / askRegistry / replyWithAsk)\\n//    stays in index.ts — that's not transformation, it's rendering.\\n//\\n//  • SCHEDULE's time-parsing dependencies (parseDuration,\\n//    parseAbsoluteTime, parseRecurrence, computeNextRunAt) are injected\\n//    via the `scheduleParsers` parameter of `transform()` so this file\\n//    doesn't pull in the whole schedule-store module graph.\\n\\nimport type { Recurrence } from \\\"./recurrence.js\\\";\\nimport { STICKER_MAP } from \\\"./stickerMap.js\\\";\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Regexes — one per token. Each is anchored with `<<NAME:` prefix and\\n// terminates at `>>`. Payload body rejects `>` and CRLF so a stray `>`\\n// inside the payload terminates cleanly instead of eating downstream\\n// tokens.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport const SEND_FILE_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_RE = /<<SCHEDULE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const ASK_RE = /<<ASK:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// 2026-05-02: reason bodies additionally reject `<` so an agent quoting\\n// other tokens inside a reason (e.g. <<GOAL_DONE:describes <<RESTART:foo>>>>)\\n// can't collapse the outer match onto the inner `>>`. SEND_FILE / SCHEDULE\\n// / ASK bodies keep `<` allowed because their payloads (paths / time specs\\n// / key=val) don't typically contain it, and banning `<` there would break\\n// legitimate payloads like URLs.\\nexport const SKILL_USED_RE =\\n  /<<SKILL_USED:\\\\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_RE = /<<GOAL_DONE:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// RESTART token (C3, 2026-05-02). `<<RESTART:reason>>` or bare\\n// `<<RESTART>>` asks the bridge to exit(1) so the supervisor respawns us.\\n// The actual guardrail (goal active? cooldown? cap?) lives in runPrompt;\\n// this regex only extracts the payload. Reason is 1-500 chars, rejects\\n// `<` / `>` / `|` / CRLF so malformed payloads (including agents quoting\\n// other tokens inside the reason) fall through as visible text instead\\n// of collapsing the match on a nested `>>`.\\nexport const RESTART_RE = /<<RESTART:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_RE = /<<RESTART>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_REASON = \\\"agent-requested-restart\\\";\\nexport const STICKER_RE = /<<STICKER:\\\\s*([a-z_]{1,20})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const CONTINUE_RE = /<<CONTINUE>>/g;\\n\\n// ASK sub-regexes (kept here because ASK payload parsing lives here too).\\nconst ASK_ID_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,32}$/;\\nconst ASK_KEY_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,20}$/;\\nconst ASK_LABEL_MAX = 40;\\n// Soft cap: labels over this width are accepted but logged. The skill\\n// (ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol) mandates >20 -> numbered body list\\n// with short labels. We do not reject here because rejecting would\\n// leave the whole token verbatim in chat; a log line gives operators\\n// something to grep when a user reports crowded/unreadable buttons.\\nconst ASK_LABEL_SOFT = 20;\\n\\n// Token-class limits (match docs/ab-token-protocol.md).\\nexport const SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX = 1000;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG = 3;\\nexport const SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG = 5;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// RESTART: only honour the first <<RESTART:...>> in one reply. Extra\\n// tokens stay in visible text as a signal to the agent (same idea as\\n// GOAL_DONE extra-token handling).\\nexport const RESTART_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// 2026-05-03: balanced-body reason cap raised from 200 → 500 and\\n// switched from hard-reject to silent truncate. The old 200-char hard reject left the token verbatim\\n// in text, goalDone=null, and the bridge kept scheduling continuation\\n// turns forever (\\\"GOAL_DONE failed to fire\\\" bug fact, 2026-05-03).\\n// Truncate keeps the token functional while preserving a sane upper\\n// bound that fits comfortably in the \\\"✓ Goal achieved: <reason>\\\" line\\n// on Telegram. Same cap applied to SKILL_USED and RESTART for policy\\n// consistency.\\nexport const BALANCED_REASON_MAX = 500;\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Payload interfaces\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport interface AskOption {\\n  key: string;\\n  label: string;\\n  recommended: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface AskEntry {\\n  questionId: string;\\n  options: AskOption[];\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ScheduleTokenEntry {\\n  runAt: number;\\n  prompt: string;\\n  whenLabel: string;\\n  recurrence?: Recurrence;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface SkillUsageEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface GoalDoneEntry {\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of a valid <<RESTART:reason>> token. Agent proposes\\n *  writing a skill; bridge shows an ASK button for user confirmation. */\\nexport interface SkillProposeEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  reason: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of a valid <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>> token. Agent decides to hand off\\n *  the user's message to a specialist in transparent proxy mode. */\\nexport interface SpecialistProxyEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  message: string;\\n}\\n\\n/** Payload of <<MOA_PLAN:{json}>> — raw single-line JSON emitted by the\\n *  aggregator in MoA plan mode (SPEC-moa-provider Phase 2). Parsing and\\n *  validation live in moa-plan.ts; this module only captures the span. */\\nexport interface MoaPlanEntry {\\n  raw: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface TransformedReply {\\n  /** Display text with all valid tokens stripped and excess blank lines collapsed. */\\n  text: string;\\n  /** Absolute paths requested via <<SEND_FILE>>. */\\n  sendFiles: string[];\\n  /** Schedules requested via <<SCHEDULE>> (only well-formed, first SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG). */\\n  scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[];\\n  /** ASK keyboards to render (de-duped by question_id). */\\n  askTokens: AskEntry[];\\n  /** De-duped skill usage reports (first SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG distinct names). */\\n  skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[];\\n  /** First GOAL_DONE token; `null` if none present. */\\n  goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null;\\n  /** First RESTART token; `null` if none present. Guardrail (goal\\n   * active? cooldown? cap? empty reason?) runs in runPrompt, not here. */\\n  restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null;\\n  /** Sticker emotion keys requested via <<STICKER:emotion>>. */\\n  stickerEmotions: string[];\\n  /** Skill proposal via <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>; null if none. */\\n  skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null;\\n  /** Wiki query page request via <<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>; null if none. */\\n  wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null;\\n  /** Whether the agent emitted <<CONTINUE>> to request another turn without a goal. */\\n  continueToken: boolean;\\n  /** Specialist proxy handoff via <<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|message>>; null if none. */\\n  specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null;\\n  /** MoA plan JSON via <<MOA_PLAN:{...}>>; null if none. First one wins. */\\n  moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null;\\n}\\n\\n/** Parsers injected by caller so this module has no dep on schedule-store. */\\nexport interface ScheduleParsers {\\n  parseDuration: (raw: string) => number | null;\\n  parseAbsoluteTime: (raw: string) => Date | null;\\n  parseRecurrence: (raw: string) => Recurrence | null;\\n  computeNextRunAt: (rec: Recurrence, after: number) => number | null;\\n  now?: () => number; // defaults to Date.now — override for tests\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Code-region masking\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Ported verbatim from index.ts's existing maskCodeRegions so refactor is\\n// behavior-preserving. Sentinel uses null bytes so nothing in normal text\\n// can collide with it.\\nconst CODE_PLACEHOLDER_RE = /\\\\u0000CODE(\\\\d+)\\\\u0000/g;\\n\\nfunction maskCodeRegions(text: string): {\\n  masked: string;\\n  restore: (s: string) => string;\\n} {\\n  const slots: string[] = [];\\n  const masked = text.replace(/```[\\\\s\\\\S]*?```|`[^`\\\\n]*`/g, (m) => {\\n    const idx = slots.length;\\n    slots.push(m);\\n    return `\\\\u0000CODE${idx}\\\\u0000`;\\n  });\\n  const restore = (s: string) =>\\n    s.replace(CODE_PLACEHOLDER_RE, (_m, i: string) => slots[Number(i)] ?? \\\"\\\");\\n  return (options.length < 1) return reject(\\\"no valid options\\\");\\n  if (options.length > 8) return reject(`too many options (got ${options.length}, max 8)`);\\n  return { questionId, options };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Balanced scanner for tokens with free-form reason bodies (GOAL_DONE /\\n// SKILL_USED / RESTART). Regex alone can't handle an agent that quotes\\n// another token inside a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:describes <<RESTART:\\n// foo>> here>>`) because non-greedy `+?` collapses on the inner `>>`\\n// and greedy `+` overshoots past the real closer. The scanner walks the\\n// string tracking `<<` / `>>` depth so outer tokens consume any nested\\n// literals before the narrower per-token regexes have a chance to see\\n// them.\\n//\\n// Design notes:\\n//   • Tokens are required to stay on a single line (spec §1.1/1.2/1.3).\\n//     A `\\\\n` inside a candidate span aborts the match and the opener\\n//     stays visible.\\n//   • Sentinel `\\\\u0000CODE<n>\\\\u0000` from code-region masking does not\\n//     contain `<<` or `>>`, so scanner is safe to run on masked text.\\n//   • Scanner is O(n) per call (single forward pass with a counter).\\n//   • `runBalancedPhase` handles all three balanced-body prefixes in\\n//     one pass so an outer `<<RESTART:...>>` containing an inner\\n//     `<<GOAL_DONE:...>>` literal is consumed atomically without the\\n//     inner token being extracted as a real GOAL_DONE.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n/** Run balanced-scanner phase for multiple token prefixes in a single\\n *  forward pass over `text`. 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. // dedup, first reason wins\\n      seenSkillNames.add(name);\\n      skillUsages.push({ name, reason });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<GOAL_DONE:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (goalDone !== null) return null;\\n      let reason = inner.trim();\\n      if (reason.length < 1) return null;\\n      if (reason.includes(\\\"|\\\")) return null;\\n      // 2026-05-03: truncate over-length reasons instead of rejecting.\\n      // Live bug: agent summaries for 10+ turn goals routinely exceeded\\n      // the old 200-char cap and the token was left verbatim, causing\\n      // goalDone=null and an infinite continuation loop.\\n      if (reason.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) reason = reason.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n      goalDone = { reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<RESTART:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (restartToken !== null) return null;\\n      let reason = inner.trim();\\n      if (reason.includes(\\\"|\\\")) return null;\\n      // 2026-05-03: truncate over-length reasons instead of rejecting.\\n      if (reason.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) reason = reason.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n      // Empty reason → use default so markRestart doesn't reject.\\n      if (!reason) reason = RESTART_BARE_REASON;\\n      restartToken = { reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SKILL_PROPOSE:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (skillPropose !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const slug = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const summary = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,80}$/.test(slug)) return null;\\n      if (!summary || summary.length > 200) return null;\\n      wikiQuery = { slug, summary };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (specialistProxy !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const message = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!message) return null;\\n      specialistProxy = { name, message };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<MOA_PLAN:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (moaPlan !== null) return null; parsers.now() : Date.now();\\n    stripped = stripped.replace(SCHEDULE_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n      if (scheduleTokens.length >= SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG) return match;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe <= 0) return match;\\n      const timeRaw = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      let prompt = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!prompt) return match;\\n      if (prompt.length > SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX) {\\n        prompt = prompt.slice(0, SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX);\\n      }\\n      const recurrence = parsers.parseRecurrence(timeRaw);\\n      if (recurrence) {\\n        const firstRunAt = parsers.computeNextRunAt(recurrence, now - 1);\\n        if (firstRunAt === null) return match;\\n        scheduleTokens.push({\\n          runAt: firstRunAt,\\n          prompt,\\n          whenLabel: timeRaw,\\n          recurrence,\\n        });\\n        return \\\"\\\";\\n      }\\n      let runAt: number | null = null;\\n      const dur = parsers.parseDuration(timeRaw);\\n      if (dur !== null) {\\n        runAt = now + dur;\\n      } else {\\n        const dt = parsers.parseAbsoluteTime(timeRaw);\\n        if (dt && dt.getTime() > now) runAt = dt.getTime();\\n      }\\n      if (runAt === null) return match;\\n      scheduleTokens.push({ runAt, prompt, whenLabel: timeRaw });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // ASK\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(ASK_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n    const entry = parseAskInner(inner);\\n    if (!entry) return match;\\n    if (seenAskIds.has(entry.questionId)) {\\n      const idx = askTokens.findIndex((a) => a.questionId === entry.questionId);\\n      if (idx >= 0) askTokens.splice(idx, 1);\\n    }\\n    seenAskIds.add(entry.questionId);\\n    askTokens.push(entry);\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Bare RESTART. Only unterminated openers are trimmed; fully-terminated\\n// tokens (malformed or not) are left alone because they've already been\\n// decided by transform()'s extractors or kept as literal text.\\n//\\n// Called from transform()'s tail so both streaming frames and the final\\n// render get the same treatment. Exported for unit testing.\\n// transform() 會處理的 token opener 名稱清單（RESTART / CONTINUE 是裸型另列）。\\n// 新增 token 時在這裡補一項——2026-07-11 前 openerRe 是手寫 regex，\\n// MOA_PLAN / SPECIALIST_PROXY 新增時漏更新，mid-write token 閃現給使用者。\\nconst TOKEN_OPENER_NAMES = [\\n  \\\"GOAL_DONE\\\", \\\"SKILL_USED\\\", \\\"SKILL_PROPOSE\\\", \\\"ASK\\\", \\\"SCHEDULE\\\",\\n  \\\"SEND_FILE\\\", \\\"STICKER\\\", \\\"WIKI_QUERY\\\", \\\"SPECIALIST_PROXY\\\", \\\"MOA_PLAN\\\",\\n];\\nconst TOKEN_OPENER_RE_SRC = `<<(?:${TOKEN_OPENER_NAMES.join(\\\"|\\\")}):|<<(?:RESTART|CONTINUE)(?::|>?$)`;\\n\\nexport function hideTrailingUnterminatedToken(text: string): string {\\n  const openerRe = new RegExp(TOKEN_OPENER_RE_SRC, \\\"g\\\");\\n  let lastOpener = -1;\\n  for (;;) {\\n    const m = openerRe.exec(text);\\n    if (!m) break;\\n    lastOpener = m.index;\\n  }\\n  if (lastOpener < 0) return text;\\n  const tail = text.slice(lastOpener);\\n  if (tail.includes(\\\">>\\\")) return text;   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\POLICIES\\\\preamble-frozen-snapshot.md\",\"content\":\"# Preamble Frozen-Snapshot Policy\\n\\n**Audience**: anyone editing `src/sessionManager.ts`, `src/memory.ts`,\\nor anything in the path that feeds Kiro / Codex / Claude's first prompt.\\n**Status**: enforced invariant, not an aspiration.\\n**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — same rationale, different\\nplumbing.\\n\\n## The invariant\\n\\nThe bridge preamble (tool notes + env block + memory block) is built\\n**once per ACP session** at session creation time. It is **never** rebuilt\\nwhile a session is live.\\n\\nConcretely:\\n\\n- `sessionManager.create()` calls `buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId)`\\n  exactly once and freezes the result into `session.memoryPreamble` +\\n  `session.preambleBreakdown`.\\n- `sessionManager.get()` returns the existing `ChatSession` object\\n  untouched when the session is still open (`!client.isClosed`). No\\n  re-read of the facts file, no re-measurement.\\n- `session.preambleInjected` is a latch: preamble text is included in\\n  the **first** `client.prompt()` call for that session and never again.\\n  Subsequent user turns send only the user text.\\n\\nThe preamble is only rebuilt when a **new session** is created, which\\nhappens in these four paths and no others:\\n\\n1. `get()` sees `existing.client.isClosed` → falls through to `create()`.\\n2. Session is idle past `config.sessionIdleMs` → `sweepIdle()` drops it\\n   and the next `get()` rebuilds.\\n3. Agent process crashes / exits unexpectedly → `exit` handler deletes\\n   the session from the map; next `get()` rebuilds.\\n4. User issues `/restart` or `/drop`, or the bridge shuts down and\\n   restarts.\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nThe Kiro / Codex / Claude ACP agents cache their system-prompt prefix\\naggressively at the LLM provider layer (Anthropic / OpenAI prompt\\ncaching, 5-minute TTL on most tiers). If the bridge were to rebuild\\npreamble mid-session and re-send it, **every user turn would invalidate\\nthe prefix cache**. For a 3–5 KB preamble that is re-hit 50 times in a\\nlong session, that's the difference between `~0 input-token cost for\\ncached content` and `full input-token cost × 50`. It can turn a cheap\\nchat into an expensive one by 1–2 orders of magnitude on the cacheable\\nportion.\\n\\nThis is the same reason Hermes Agent's docs hammer the point (see their\\n`prompt-assembly.md` and the `# Prompt Caching Must Not Break` policy\\nin their AGENTS.md): **the only time we alter the cached prefix is when\\nwe're already throwing the cache away** (i.e. starting a new session).\\n\\n## Forbidden mid-session operations\\n\\nDo **not** add code that:\\n\\n- Calls `buildPreamble*()` after `create()` has returned.\\n- Mutates `session.memoryPreamble` / `session.preambleBreakdown` in place.\\n- Re-reads `facts-<userId>.md` on every turn to \\\"keep memory fresh\\\".\\n- Reloads `topics.json` / `notebooklm-routing.json` and re-injects them\\n  into the live preamble.\\n- Hot-reloads SKILL.md contents into the system prompt.\\n- Resets `session.preambleInjected = false` to \\\"re-inject\\\" newer context.\\n\\nIf you think you need any of the above, you almost certainly want a new\\nsession instead — drop the old one (`session.client.close()` and\\n`this.sessions.delete(chatId)`) so the next `get()` rebuilds. That's\\nthe right way to invalidate cache.\\n\\n## Allowed mid-session operations\\n\\nThese are fine because they don't touch the cached prefix:\\n\\n- Writing to disk: `remember()` / `forget()` / scheduler state / queued\\n  messages / history → all legitimate, all mid-session-safe.\\n- Updating ephemeral state on the `ChatSession` object that is not part\\n  of the preamble: `buffer`, `toolLog`, `charsIn/Out`, `usage`,\\n  `stderrTail`, `inflight`, `lastUsed`.\\n- Appending content to the **current user turn** via tool outputs or\\n  MCP responses. Those are post-prefix, not cached.\\n- Scheduler-triggered commands (`/memorytoskill`, `/topicreview`, etc.)\\n  — these open their own ephemeral ACP client and never touch the\\n  live user session.\\n\\n## Practical consequence for memory writes\\n\\nWhen the agent (or the bridge, on the user's behalf) calls `remember()`\\nmid-session:\\n\\n1. The fact is appended to `facts-<userId>.md` **immediately**.\\n2. The topic shard at `facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md` is updated\\n   **immediately**.\\n3. The live preamble is **not** updated.\\n4. The agent will see the new fact next time the session is rebuilt —\\n   i.e. after `/restart`, or after `sessionIdleMs` elapses, or after a\\n   manual `/drop`.\\n\\nThis is **by design**, not a bug. Document any user-facing \\\"I'll\\nremember that for next time\\\" phrasing accordingly.\\n\\n## If you really need mid-session awareness\\n\\nThe correct escape hatches, in order of preference:\\n\\n1. **Tool response**: let the agent call an MCP tool (e.g. `list_facts`)\\n   to query the live state. Tool responses are not cached and always\\n   see disk.\\n2. **Next-turn user message**: queue a synthetic next-turn message like\\n   \\\"Note: just added to memory: …\\\" — this appends after the cached\\n   prefix and is fine.\\n3. **End the session**: if the change is significant enough to need to\\n   be in the prefix, `/restart` is cheap and correct.\\n\\nWhat you **must not** do is rebuild the preamble silently. That breaks\\nthe invariant this document exists to protect.\\n\\n## Change-this-policy checklist\\n\\nBefore any PR that touches `buildPreamble*`, `memoryPreamble`,\\n`preambleInjected`, or the `SessionManager.create()` codepath:\\n\\n- [ ] Confirm the change does not cause preamble to be re-emitted on any\\n  turn other than the first of a new session.\\n- [ ] Grep for any new caller of `buildPreamble*()` outside\\n  `sessionManager.ts` — if there is one, it's suspect.\\n- [ ] If the change rebuilds sessions more often (e.g. lowers\\n  `sessionIdleMs`, adds a reason for reset), explicitly justify it\\n  against the prompt-cache cost.\\n- [ ] If you remove or weaken `preambleInjected` latch semantics,\\n  replace this document with an up-to-date one (or delete it and amend\\n  `AGENTS.md`).\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- `src/sessionManager.ts` — `create()` is the canonical build point.\\n- `src/memory.ts` — `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` is the only preamble\\n  builder.\\n- `.kiro/steering/windows-shell-safety.md` — sibling steering doc.\\n- Hermes Agent docs, `developer-guide/prompt-assembly.md` — external\\n  reference for the same pattern in a different codebase.\\n\",\"numLines\":143,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":143}}"</outcome>

\\\"${raw}\\\" missing \\\"key=label\\\"`);\\n    const key = spec.slice(0, eq).trim();\\n    const label = spec.slice(eq + 1).trim();\\n    if (!ASK_KEY_RE.test(key)) {\\n      return reject(\\n \\\"${key}\\\" label too long (got ${label.length}, max ${ASK_LABEL_MAX}): \\\"${label}\\\"`,\\n      );\\n    }\\n    if (keys.has(key)) return reject(`duplicate key \\\"${key}\\\"`);\\n    keys.add(key);\\n    if (recommended && recommendedCount > 0) recommended = false;\\n    if (recommended) recommendedCount++;\\n    options.push({ key, label, recommended });\\n  }\\n  if (options.length < 1) return reject(\\\"no valid options\\\");\\n  if (options.length > 8) return reject(`too many options (got ${options.length}, max 8)`);\\n  return { questionId, options };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Balanced scanner for tokens with free-form reason bodies (GOAL_DONE /\\n// SKILL_USED / RESTART). Regex alone can't handle an agent that quotes\\n// another token inside a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:describes <<RESTART:\\n// foo>> here>>`) because non-greedy `+?` collapses on the inner `>>`\\n// and greedy `+` overshoots past the real closer. The scanner walks the\\n// string tracking `<<` / `>>` depth so outer tokens consume any nested\\n// literals before the narrower per-token regexes have a chance to see\\n// them.\\n//\\n// Design notes:\\n//   • Tokens are required to stay on a single line (spec §1.1/1.2/1.3).\\n//     A `\\\\n` inside a candidate span aborts the match and the opener\\n//     stays visible.\\n//   • Sentinel `\\\\u0000CODE<n>\\\\u0000` from code-region masking does not\\n//     contain `<<` or `>>`, so scanner is safe to run on masked text.\\n//   • Scanner is O(n) per call (single forward pass with a counter).\\n//   • `runBalancedPhase` handles all three balanced-body prefixes in\\n//     one pass so an outer `<<RESTART:...>>` containing an inner\\n//     `<<GOAL_DONE:...>>` literal is consumed atomically without the\\n//     inner token being extracted as a real GOAL_DONE.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n/** Run balanced-scanner phase for multiple token prefixes in a single\\n *  forward pass over `text`. At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. Tokens inside\\n * code regions are ignored. Malformed tokens are left in the visible\\n * text as documentation for the agent. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\\n  // Collect payloads in a mutable object while we do the .replace passes.\\n  const sendFiles: string[] = [];\\n  const scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[] = [];\\n  const askTokens: AskEntry[] = [];\\n  const skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[] = [];\\n  const seenSkillNames = new Set<string>();\\n  const seenAskIds = new Set<string>();\\n  let goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null = null;\\n  let restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null = null;\\n  let skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null = null;\\n  let wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null = null;\\n  let specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null = null;\\n  let moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null = null;\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 1 — balanced-scanner tokens (free-form reason bodies).\\n  // These run FIRST so that when an agent nests another token inside\\n  // a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:... <<RESTART:foo>> ...>>`), the outer\\n  // match consumes the whole span including the nested literal. Later\\n  // phases never see the inner token and can't trigger it by accident.\\n  //\\n  // Single forward pass over `masked`: at each `<<PREFIX:` (for any of\\n  // the three balanced-body token classes), we walk to the matching\\n  // top-level `>>` and hand the inner text to the right handler.\\n  // Crucially, the walk happens before any narrower extractor runs —\\n  // nested `<<...>>` inside a reason is consumed as part of the outer\\n  // span and never appears as an independent token.\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  let stripped = runBalancedPhase(masked, {\\n    \\\"<<SKILL_USED:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (skillUsages.length >= SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG) return null;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      let reason = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      // 2026-05-03: truncate over-length reasons instead of rejecting.\\n      // See BALANCED_REASON_MAX comment for rationale.\\n      if (reason.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) reason = reason.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n      if (seenSkillNames.has(name)) return \\\"\\\"; // dedup, first reason wins\\n      seenSkillNames.add(name);\\n      skillUsages.push({ name, reason });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<GOAL_DONE:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (goalDone !== null) return null;\\n      let reason = inner.trim();\\n      if (reason.length < 1) return null;\\n      if (reason.includes(\\\"|\\\")) return null;\\n      // 2026-05-03: truncate over-length reasons instead of rejecting.\\n      // Live bug: agent summaries for 10+ turn goals routinely exceeded\\n      // the old 200-char cap and the token was left verbatim, causing\\n      // goalDone=null and an infinite continuation loop.\\n      if (reason.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) reason = reason.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n      goalDone = { reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<RESTART:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (restartToken !== null) return null;\\n      let reason = inner.trim();\\n      if (reason.includes(\\\"|\\\")) return null;\\n      // 2026-05-03: truncate over-length reasons instead of rejecting.\\n      if (reason.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) reason = reason.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n      // Empty reason → use default so markRestart doesn't reject.\\n      if (!reason) reason = RESTART_BARE_REASON;\\n      restartToken = { reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SKILL_PROPOSE:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (skillPropose !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const reason = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!reason || reason.length > 500) return null;\\n      skillPropose = { name, reason };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<WIKI_QUERY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (wikiQuery !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const slug = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const summary = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-z0-9_-]{1,80}$/.test(slug)) return null;\\n      if (!summary || summary.length > 200) return null;\\n      wikiQuery = { slug, summary };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (specialistProxy !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      const message = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60}$/.test(name)) return null;\\n      if (!message) return null;\\n      specialistProxy = { name, message };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n    \\\"<<MOA_PLAN:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (moaPlan !== null) return null; // only first one\\n      const raw = inner.trim();\\n      // Must at least look like a JSON object — anything else stays\\n      // visible so the aggregator sees its own malformed emission.\\n      // Balanced-scanner constraints (single line, no bare `>>` inside)\\n      // are part of the token contract in the plan-mode prompt.\\n      if (!raw.startsWith(\\\"{\\\") || !raw.endsWith(\\\"}\\\")) return null;\\n      moaPlan = { raw };\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    },\\n  });\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 2 — regex-based tokens (structured bodies: path / time-spec\\n  // / id|k=v). These don't nest other tokens so plain regex is fine.\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  // Pre-fix: repair SEND_FILE paths broken by JSON deserialization.\\n  // When the agent emits backslash Windows paths (F:\\\\AI\\\\...), JSON\\n  // serialization in the CLI turns \\\\r → CR, \\\\n → LF, \\\\t → TAB, etc.\\n  // This makes the regex (which stops at \\\\r\\\\n) unable to capture the\\n  // full path. 會讓未閉合的\\n  // opener 貪到後文任意 `>>`（如另一個 token 的結尾），吞掉中間正文。\\n  const SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([^<>]{1,300}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_REPAIR_RE, (_m, body: string) => {\\n    const repaired = body\\n      .replace(/\\\\r/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\r\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\n/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\")\\n      .replace(/\\\\t/g, \\\"\\\\\\\\t\\\");\\n    return `<<SEND_FILE:${repaired}>>`;\\n  });\\n\\n  // SEND_FILE\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(SEND_FILE_RE, (_m, p: string) => {\\n    sendFiles.push(p.trim());\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // STICKER\\n  const stickerEmotions: string[] = [];\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(STICKER_RE, (_m, emotion: string) => {\\n    const key = emotion.trim().toLowerCase();\\n    if (STICKER_MAP[key] && !stickerEmotions.includes(key)) {\\n      stickerEmotions.push(key);\\n    }\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // SCHEDULE (only if parsers provided)\\n  if (parsers) {\\n    const now = parsers.now ? parsers.now() : Date.now();\\n    stripped = stripped.replace(SCHEDULE_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n      if (scheduleTokens.length >= SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG) return match;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe <= 0) return match;\\n      const timeRaw = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n      let prompt = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n      if (!prompt) return match;\\n      if (prompt.length > SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX) {\\n        prompt = prompt.slice(0, SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX);\\n      }\\n      const recurrence = parsers.parseRecurrence(timeRaw);\\n      if (recurrence) {\\n        const firstRunAt = parsers.computeNextRunAt(recurrence, now - 1);\\n        if (firstRunAt === null) return match;\\n        scheduleTokens.push({\\n          runAt: firstRunAt,\\n          prompt,\\n          whenLabel: timeRaw,\\n          recurrence,\\n        });\\n        return \\\"\\\";\\n      }\\n      let runAt: number | null = null;\\n      const dur = parsers.parseDuration(timeRaw);\\n      if (dur !== null) {\\n        runAt = now + dur;\\n      } else {\\n        const dt = parsers.parseAbsoluteTime(timeRaw);\\n        if (dt && dt.getTime() > now) runAt = dt.getTime();\\n      }\\n      if (runAt === null) return match;\\n      scheduleTokens.push({ runAt, prompt, whenLabel: timeRaw });\\n      return \\\"\\\";\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // ASK\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(ASK_RE, (match, inner: string) => {\\n    const entry = parseAskInner(inner);\\n    if (!entry) return match;\\n    if (seenAskIds.has(entry.questionId)) {\\n      const idx = askTokens.findIndex((a) => a.questionId === entry.questionId);\\n      if (idx >= 0) askTokens.splice(idx, 1);\\n    }\\n    seenAskIds.add(entry.questionId);\\n    askTokens.push(entry);\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Bare RESTART. Keep this after the balanced phase so `<<RESTART:...>>`\\n  // retains nested-token handling, while the short host-side form also works.\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(RESTART_BARE_RE, (match) => {\\n    if (restartToken !== null) return match;\\n    restartToken = { reason: RESTART_BARE_REASON };\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // CONTINUE — bare token, no payload. Agent signals \\\"I have more work\\n  // to do, please give me another turn\\\" without requiring an active /goal.\\n  let continueToken = false;\\n  stripped = stripped.replace(CONTINUE_RE, () => {\\n    continueToken = true;\\n    return \\\"\\\";\\n  });\\n\\n  // Hide trailing unterminated token opener (see helper docs below).\\n  // 2026-05-02: originally this ran only in renderReply() for streaming\\n  // frames, under the assumption final render always receives a fully-\\n  // closed token set. Running hide on transform()'s cleaned output makes\\n  // both paths (streaming + final) share the same UX guarantee.\\n  const hidden = hideTrailingUnterminatedToken(\\n    restore(stripped).replace(/\\\\n{3,}/g, \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\").trim(),\\n  );\\n\\n  return {\\n    text: hidden,\\n    sendFiles,\\n    scheduleTokens,\\n    askTokens,\\n    skillUsages,\\n    goalDone,\\n    restartToken,\\n    stickerEmotions,\\n    skillPropose,\\n    wikiQuery,\\n    continueToken,\\n    specialistProxy,\\n    moaPlan,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Streaming UX helper: hide a trailing unterminated token opener.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// When the agent is mid-write on a token like `<<GOAL_DONE:reason still\\n// being typed…`, transform() can't match the token (no closing `>>`)\\n// and leaves the opener visible in the output text. The user then sees\\n// `<<GOAL_DONE:reason still…` flicker in the placeholder until the\\n// agent emits `>>` — or worse, sees it frozen if the stream ends\\n// mid-token (final render also inherits the raw opener).\\n//\\n// This helper finds the LAST known-prefix opener in `text` that has no\\n// matching `>>` after it, and replaces the span from that opener to EOF\\n// with `…`. Only unterminated openers are trimmed; fully-terminated\\n// tokens (malformed or not) are left alone because they've already been\\n// decided by transform()'s extractors or kept as literal text.\\n//\\n// Called from transform()'s tail so both streaming frames and the final\\n// render get the same treatment. Exported for unit testing.\\n// transform() 會處理的 token opener 名稱清單（RESTART / CONTINUE 是裸型另列）。\\n// 新增 token 時在這裡補一項——2026-07-11 前 openerRe 是手寫 regex，\\n// MOA_PLAN / SPECIALIST_PROXY 新增時漏更新，mid-write token 閃現給使用者。\\nconst TOKEN_OPENER_NAMES = [\\n  \\\"GOAL_DONE\\\", \\\"SKILL_USED\\\", \\\"SKILL_PROPOSE\\\", \\\"ASK\\\", \\\"SCHEDULE\\\",\\n  \\\"SEND_FILE\\\", \\\"STICKER\\\", \\\"WIKI_QUERY\\\", \\\"SPECIALIST_PROXY\\\", \\\"MOA_PLAN\\\",\\n];\\nconst TOKEN_OPENER_RE_SRC = `<<(?:${TOKEN_OPENER_NAMES.join(\\\"|\\\")}):|<<(?:RESTART|CONTINUE)(?::|>?$)`;\\n\\nexport function hideTrailingUnterminatedToken(text: string): string {\\n  const openerRe = new RegExp(TOKEN_OPENER_RE_SRC, \\\"g\\\");\\n  let lastOpener = -1;\\n  for (;;) {\\n    const m = openerRe.exec(text);\\n    if (!m) break;\\n    lastOpener = m.index;\\n  }\\n  if (lastOpener < 0) return text;\\n  const tail = text.slice(lastOpener);\\n  if (tail.includes(\\\">>\\\")) return text; RESTART added 2026-05-02 (C3) for agent-\\ndriven mid-goal restarts; see §1.3.\\n**Depends on**: `.kiro/steering/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md` (C policy).\\n**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent's `/goal` + Curator `.usage.json`, adapted\\nto our bridge-not-agent architecture.\\n\\n## 1. New tokens\\n\\n### 1.1 `<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`\\n\\n**Emitted by**: agent, in its streamed reply, when it believes the\\ncurrent `/goal` objective is complete.\\n\\n**Syntax**:\\n```\\n<<GOAL_DONE:完成理由>>\\n```\\n\\n- Single line, MUST NOT span newlines.\\n- `reason`: plain text, ≤ 500 chars (over-length reasons are\\n  **silently truncated** to 500 chars, NOT rejected — see\\n  `BALANCED_REASON_MAX` in `observerTransformer.ts`). MUST NOT\\n  contain `|` or CR/LF. May\\n  contain `<` / `>` only when they form balanced `<<…>>` pairs — the\\n  scanner tracks depth so an agent can safely describe other tokens\\n  inside the reason (e.g.\\n  `<<GOAL_DONE:verified <<RESTART:foo>> end-to-end>>` captures the\\n  whole span atomically and the inner `<<RESTART:foo>>` does NOT fire\\n  as a real restart). Used verbatim in the `✓ Goal achieved` bridge\\n  message.\\n- At most **1 occurrence per reply** — bridge honors the first and\\n  treats the rest as garbage.\\n\\n**Semantics**: presence = \\\"I believe the goal is done\\\". Absence =\\n\\\"keep going\\\". There is no false variant — the simple model is\\n[Hermes's philosophy](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/goals#the-judge) applied to a\\nbridge architecture: fail-open, budget-bounded, simple to get right.\\n\\n**Regex (legacy format reference — actual extractor is a balanced\\n`<<`/`>>` scanner that handles nested tokens inside reason bodies.\\nNote: the handler truncates >500-char reasons, so agents don't need\\nto self-check length — the old 200-char hard reject caused a live\\nbug where agents wrote long \\\"why we're done\\\" summaries and the\\ntoken stayed verbatim, leaving the goal active in an infinite\\ncontinuation loop. Fixed 2026-05-03)**:\\n```ts\\nconst GOAL_DONE_RE = /<<GOAL_DONE:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n```\\n\\n**Bridge behavior**:\\n- Token appears, goal **active** → strip token, stop continuation loop,\\n  send `✓ Goal achieved: <reason>` line, clear goal state.\\n- Token appears, goal **not active** → strip token, log warn, reply\\n  unchanged otherwise.\\n- No token, goal active, turn budget remaining → auto-emit\\n  continuation user-turn (see §3).\\n- No token, goal active, turn budget exhausted → pause loop, send\\n  `⏸ Goal paused — N/N turns used` message.\\n\\n**Position hint**: agent should place the token in the **last few\\nlines** of its reply. The bridge does not enforce position, but\\nreasoning in the tail reduces accidental self-cancellation from\\nin-text examples.\\n\\n### 1.2 `<<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>`\\n\\n**Emitted by**: agent, whenever it **actually consulted** a skill in\\n`${SKILL_DIR}` to produce its reply.\\n\\n**Syntax**:\\n```\\n<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|一句話理由>>\\n```\\n\\n- Single line.\\n- `name`: skill directory name. Charset `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`, ≤ 60 chars.\\n- `reason`: plain text, ≤ 500 chars (over-length reasons **silently\\n  truncated** to 500, not rejected — policy consistency with\\n  GOAL_DONE/RESTART). MUST NOT contain `|` or CRLF. May\\n  contain `<` / `>` when they form balanced `<<…>>` pairs — the\\n  scanner consumes the outer span atomically so a nested\\n  `<<SEND_FILE:...>>` (or any other token literal) inside the reason\\n  stays in the reason and is NOT extracted as a real side-effect.\\n- Up to **5 tokens per reply** (chosen value, see `AB-1` ASK).\\n  Tokens #6+ in a single reply are left in text as-is so the agent\\n  notices the limit.\\n- Duplicates (same `name` twice in one reply) are de-duped — counted\\n  as one `use_count++`, first `reason` wins.\\n\\n**Regex**:\\n```ts\\nconst SKILL_USED_RE =\\n  /<<SKILL_USED:\\\\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500})\\\\s*>>/g;\\n```\\n\\nMismatch (name with `.`, pipe in reason, etc.) → token left in text,\\nnot stripped, not counted. Agent sees its own garbage. Over-length\\nreasons are truncated to 500 chars rather than rejected.\\n\\n**Bridge behavior**:\\n- Strip all well-formed tokens.\\n- For each surviving token:\\n  - If `name` exists in `${SKILL_DIR}/<name>/SKILL.md` → update\\n    `.usage.json[<name>]`: `use_count++`, `last_agent_used_at = now`,\\n    `last_reason = reason`.\\n  - If not → create `.usage.json[<name>] = { orphan: true, ... }`\\n    (flagged but not rejected — agent might be citing a renamed/\\n    deleted skill and the user should see that).\\n\\n**Semantics**: \\\"I read or followed this SKILL.md to help with this\\nreply\\\". Background-knowledge alignment (\\\"I know Kiro well\\\") is **not**\\na SKILL_USED event.   <next_steps>Workflow is running in background and will automatically notify when complete with design specification. User can monitor progress with `/workflows` command</next_steps>
  <notes>The question suggests adding a quality gate similar to those in uk-slot-spec-to-impl skill (which has checklist gates). </summary>

The bridge does not enforce position, but\\nreasoning in the tail reduces accidental self-cancellation from\\nin-text examples.\\n\\n### 1.2 `<<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>`\\n\\n**Emitted by**: agent, whenever it **actually consulted** a skill in\\n`${SKILL_DIR}` to produce its reply.\\n\\n**Syntax**:\\n```\\n<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|一句話理由>>\\n```\\n\\n- Single line.\\n- `name`: skill directory name. Charset `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`, ≤ 60 chars.\\n- `reason`: plain text, ≤ 500 chars (over-length reasons **silently\\n  truncated** to 500, not rejected — policy consistency with\\n  GOAL_DONE/RESTART). MUST NOT contain `|` or CRLF. May\\n  contain `<` / `>` when they form balanced `<<…>>` pairs — the\\n  scanner consumes the outer span atomically so a nested\\n  `<<SEND_FILE:...>>` (or any other token literal) inside the reason\\n  stays in the reason and is NOT extracted as a real side-effect.\\n- Up to **5 tokens per reply** (chosen value, see `AB-1` ASK).\\n  Tokens #6+ in a single reply are left in text as-is so the agent\\n  notices the limit.\\n- Duplicates (same `name` twice in one reply) are de-duped — counted\\n  as one `use_count++`, first `reason` wins.\\n\\n**Regex**:\\n```ts\\nconst SKILL_USED_RE =\\n  /<<SKILL_USED:\\\\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500})\\\\s*>>/g;\\n```\\n\\nMismatch (name with `.`, pipe in reason, etc.) → token left in text,\\nnot stripped, not counted. Agent sees its own garbage. Over-length\\nreasons are truncated to 500 chars rather than rejected.\\n\\n**Bridge behavior**:\\n- Strip all well-formed tokens.\\n- For each surviving token:\\n  - If `name` exists in `${SKILL_DIR}/<name>/SKILL.md` → update\\n    `.usage.json[<name>]`: `use_count++`, `last_agent_used_at = now`,\\n    `last_reason = reason`.\\n  - If not → create `.usage.json[<name>] = { orphan: true, ... }`\\n    (flagged but not rejected — agent might be citing a renamed/\\n    deleted skill and the user should see that).\\n\\n**Semantics**: \\\"I read or followed this SKILL.md to help with this\\nreply\\\". Background-knowledge alignment (\\\"I know Kiro well\\\") is **not**\\na SKILL_USED event. The preamble note (§4) instructs accordingly.\\n\\n### 1.3 `<<RESTART:reason>>`\\n\",\"numLines\":120,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":592}}"</outcome>

Preamble 每個 session 只 build 一次並凍結（POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md），三個 backend 收到同一份文字，中途不可重建。\\n2. Claude Code 專屬機制（Skill 工具呼叫、Workflow 多 agent 編排、PreToolUse Hook 強制擋修改）對 Kiro CLI / Codex backend 無效——PreToolUse Hook 跨 CLI 投影已明確否決過（decision-gate-hook-minimal）。\\n3. 目前唯一真正跨三個 backend 生效的機制是「純文字層指令 + 裸 token 協定」：模型讀 preamble 文字自律照做，回覆中裸寫 token（如既有 `<<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>`），bridge 端 `src/observerTransformer.ts` 用正則抓取記錄，不依賴任何 CLI 專屬工具。\\n4. 現有品質機制都是質性判定，非量化：Section 9 合理性審查（5 點自問）、Section 11 CLI 是否真的會遵守純文字指令等）。\\n3. 最終設計規格（含 BC-x 可驗證行為契約），明確標出要改動的檔案（preamble builder、observerTransformer.ts、sessionManager.ts 或等效覆核邏輯、CLAUDE.md 方法論新增章節）與各檔案改動範圍。\\n\\n這是方法論修改（CLAUDE.md 新增章節）+ 跨 3+ 檔案的大型任務，需要完整 級（檔:行原文）/ 🟡 B 級（間接）/ 🔴 反例檢查（若真應見X·若假應見Y·實際Z）。\\n   只有 A 級或 B 級+反例通過才可當設計前提；弱證據必須標「待驗證」，不可當既成事實設計。\\n2. 因果鏈（依賴影響）：設計觸及既有程式碼時，標出受影響的連動點（grep 呼叫者窮舉），\\n   呼叫者=0 的修改點要警示（可能有 inline 繞過）。\\n3.   constraints: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },\\n    unknowns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: '需求中未指定、需澄清的點' },\\n    factClaims: { type: 'array', items: { =>\\n  agent(\\n    `你是架構設計 agent（${a.key} 視角）：${a.brief}\\n需求：「${REQUIREMENT}」\\n探索結果：${CTX}\\n產出一個完整架構方案：核心取向 / 模組與職責 / BC-x 行為契約（≥ 2，可驗證）/ 取捨與風險 / 因果鏈觸及的既有連動點。\\n${LOADBEARING}`,\\n    { label: `propose:${a.key}`, phase: 'Propose', schema: DESIGN_SCHEMA }\\n  )\\n))).filter(Boolean)\\n\\n// ============================================================\\nphase('Adversarial')\\n\\nconst JUDGE_SCHEMA = {\\n  type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,\\n  required: ['rankings', 'fatalFlaws', 'recommendation'],\\n  properties: {\\n    rankings: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, required: ['approach', 'score', 'why'], properties: { approach: { type: 'string' }, score: { type: 'number' }, why: { type: 'string' } } } },\\n    fatalFlaws: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string', description: '任一方案的致命缺陷' } },\\n    recommendation: { type: 'string', description: '推薦哪個方案為基底 + 該嫁接其他方案的哪些亮點' },\\n  },\\n}\\n\\n// 對每個方案派 skeptic 找致命缺陷（perspective-diverse），再 judge-panel 評分\\nconst critiques = (await parallel(proposals.map((p, i) => () =>\\n  agent(\\n    `你是設計缺陷 skeptic。盡力找出以下架構方案的致命缺陷（過度設計 / 遺漏邊界 / 因果鏈漏接 / 事實前提錯誤 /
