judgment = await agent(\\n  `你是設計評審團。根據 ${proposals.length} 個方案 + 各自的 skeptic 批評，評分排名、列出跨方案的致命缺陷、推薦基底方案並指出該嫁接哪些亮點。\\n方案：${JSON.stringify(proposals)}\\n批評：${JSON.stringify(critiques)}`,\\n  { label: 'judge-panel', phase: 'Adversarial', schema: JUDGE_SCHEMA }\\n)\\n\\n// ============================================================\\nphase('Synthesize')\\n\\nconst spec = await agent(\\n  `你是首席架構師，產出最終設計規格（繁中，直接落檔到 .claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md）。\\n從評審推薦的基底方案綜合，嫁接亞軍亮點，吸收 skeptic 指出的缺陷做修正。\\n需求：「${REQUIREMENT}」\\n探索：${CTX}\\n方案：${JSON.stringify(proposals)}\\n評審：${JSON.stringify(judgment)}\\n\\n設計規格須含：目標 / 模組與職責 / BC-x 行為契約（可驗證，編號）/ 因果鏈（觸及的既有連動點 + 影響決策）/ 取捨與風險 / 學習查詢結果（若有讀到 .claudedocs/records/問題追蹤.md 長期警惕模式）。\\n${LOADBEARING}\\n用 Write 把規格寫入 .claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md（目錄不存在先建），並在回報摘要設計要點 + 殘餘風險。`,\\n  { label: 'synthesize-spec', phase: 'Synthesize' }\\n)\\n\\nreturn { spec, judgment }\\n\"}"</parameters>

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Current Quality Control Mechanisms in Bridge Token System</title>
<subtitle>Bridge uses goal completion and restart tokens but lacks numeric self-scoring with automatic re-work thresholds</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>telegram-kiro-bridge implements AB Token Protocol with GOAL_DONE, SKILL_USED, and RESTART tokens for workflow control</fact>
<fact>GOAL_DONE token allows agent to signal task completion with reason (≤500 chars), triggering goal state clearance</fact>
<fact>RESTART token enables agent-driven session restarts with reason validation and caps</fact>
<fact>Token system uses balanced-body scanner to handle nested token literals inside reason fields without accidental triggering</fact>
<fact>No numeric self-evaluation scoring system (e.g., 95-point threshold) exists in current ab-token-protocol.md or observerTransformer.ts</fact>
<fact>Preamble-frozen-snapshot policy enforces cache-conscious session design by building preamble once per session at creation time</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The investigation revealed that telegram-kiro-bridge uses a token-based quality control system documented in docs/ab-token-protocol.md and implemented in src/observerTransformer.ts. The system includes GOAL_DONE for task completion signaling, SKILL_USED for skill usage tracking, and RESTART for mid-goal session restarts. All tokens use balanced-body scanning to prevent nested token literals from accidentally firing. However, there is no numeric self-evaluation mechanism where the agent scores its work and automatically re-modifies if the score falls below a threshold (such as ≤95 points). The current system is binary (done/not-done) rather than scored, and relies on the agent's judgment via reason strings rather than quantitative quality gates. The preamble-frozen-snapshot policy ensures session preambles are built once and never rebuilt mid-session to preserve prompt cache efficiency, which would need consideration for any self-evaluation mechanism that requires mid-session quality checks.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>what-changed</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
</concepts>

content.thinking:
The user is asking about whether a self-evaluation mechanism exists that re-modifies work if the score is ≤95 points. The workflow has been launched to design such a system.

Now I'm observing:
1. A workflow was launched (dev-design) to create a quantitative self-evaluation system for telegram-kiro-bridge
2. The workflow will explore multiple competing design approaches for adding numeric scoring (e.g., 95-point threshold)
3. The requirement is to have a unified process across three different ACP backends (Kiro CLI, Codex, Claude Agent)
4. Current system only has qualitative checks (reasonableness review, adversarial verification) but no numeric scoring
5. The design must respect preamble-frozen-snapshot policy and work across all three backends using text-based token protocol

This is a discovery/design phase - no actual implementation yet, just launching the design workflow.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Workflow Launched to Design Numeric Self-Evaluation System</title>
<subtitle>dev-design workflow initiated to create quantitative scoring mechanism with 95-point threshold for automatic re-work across three ACP backends</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>No numeric self-evaluation scoring system currently exists in telegram-kiro-bridge codebase</fact>
<fact>dev-design workflow launched with taskId wej302c6m to design scoring system across Kiro CLI, Codex, and Claude Agent backends</fact>
<fact>Design requirement includes six-dimension checklist scoring (type validation 25pts, functional verification 20pts, caller impact 20pts, scope discipline 15pts, completeness 10pts, read-back verification 10pts)</fact>
<fact>Proposed threshold: ≥80 passes, 60-79 triggers targeted rework (max 2 rounds), <60 triggers existing circuit-breaker mechanism</fact>
<fact>Design must work via text-based token protocol (new <<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>> token) since PreToolUse hooks only work in Claude Agent backend</fact>
<fact>Workflow will produce at least 3 competing design approaches with adversarial verification to find fatal flaws</fact>
<fact>preamble-frozen-snapshot policy constraint requires design to work with preamble built once per session without mid-session rebuilding</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>A dev-design workflow was launched to address the absence of numeric self-evaluation in telegram-kiro-bridge. Currently, the system only has qualitative quality controls (Section 9 reasonableness review, Section 11 fact-claim gates, R-5 needs-attention circuit breaker). The workflow will design a quantitative scoring mechanism where the agent evaluates its code changes across six dimensions (type validation, functional verification, caller impact, scope discipline, completeness, read-back verification) with a target threshold of ≥80 points to pass. The key challenge is making this work uniformly across three different ACP backends (Kiro CLI, Codex, Claude Agent) since only text-based preamble instructions and bare token protocol work across all three. The workflow follows a four-phase process: Explore (discover existing architecture and constraints), Propose (generate 3+ competing design approaches from MVP-first, robustness-first, and simplicity-first perspectives), Adversarial (skeptics find fatal flaws in each proposal, then judge-panel ranks them), and Synthesize (produce final design spec with BC-x behavior contracts). The design must respect the preamble-frozen-snapshot policy which prevents mid-session preamble rebuilding for prompt cache efficiency.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>problem-solution</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>trade-off</concept>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
</concepts>

content.thinking:
The user is exploring the codebase to understand how skill directories and working directories are detected and configured. This is likely part of the workflow's "Explore" phase where it's gathering information about the existing architecture.

The grep results show:
1. `detectSkillDir()` and `detectAgentConfigDir()` functions are used to find skill directories
2. `sessionCwd` is used extensively throughout the codebase as the working directory for various operations
3. The working directory is passed to ACP client initialization

This exploration is part of understanding how the self-evaluation mechanism would need to integrate with the existing architecture - particularly where code changes happen (sessionCwd) and where skills are stored (detectSkillDir()).

I should document this as part of the discovery/exploration phase of the design work.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Working Directory and Skill Directory Architecture Discovery</title>
<subtitle>sessionCwd serves as the working directory for all ACP operations, with detectSkillDir() dynamically resolving skill storage location per backend</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>config.sessionCwd is the centralized working directory used across 38+ locations in src/ for ACP client initialization, specialist creation, script execution, and file operations</fact>
<fact>detectSkillDir() function in src/memory.ts dynamically detects skill directory location based on agent configuration, supporting different backends</fact>
<fact>detectAgentConfigDir() function resolves agent-specific config directory, used as input to detectSkillDir()</fact>
<fact>sessionManager.ts passes sessionCwd to ACP client initialization for both Kiro CLI and Claude Agent backends</fact>
<fact>Working directory is injected into specialist environments via BRIDGE_DIR environment variable resolved from sessionCwd</fact>
<fact>skillUsages token processing uses detectSkillDir() to locate skill directory before calling usageStore.markUsed()</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The exploration phase uncovered the working directory architecture critical for designing a self-evaluation system. The config.sessionCwd serves as the canonical working directory for all ACP backend operations, appearing in 38+ locations across the codebase for client initialization, specialist spawning, script execution, and file I/O. The skill directory location is dynamically detected via detectSkillDir() which takes an optional agentConfigDir parameter, allowing the system to adapt to different backend configurations (Kiro CLI uses ~/.kiro/skills, Claude Agent uses ~/.claude/skills). This dynamic resolution is already integrated into the token processing pipeline - when SKILL_USED tokens are extracted by observerTransformer.ts, the bridge calls detectSkillDir() to locate the skill directory before recording usage. For a self-evaluation mechanism that needs to work across all three backends, understanding this working directory architecture is essential - any code verification (like running `npx tsc --noEmit`) would need to execute from sessionCwd, and any skill-based evaluation logic would need to use detectSkillDir() rather than hardcoded paths.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
</concepts>

Token 協定層（src/observerTransformer.ts，761行）：單一抽取入口 transform()，分兩階段——Phase 1 balanced-scanner 處理『自由格式 reason body』token（SKILL_USED / GOAL_DONE / RESTART / SKILL_PROPOSE / WIKI_QUERY / SPECIALIST_PROXY / MOA_PLAN），能正確處理巢狀 token（reason 裡引用其他 token 字面）；Phase 2 regex-based 處理『結構化 body』token（SEND_FILE / SCHEDULE / ASK / STICKER）。所有 token 都有 code-region masking（避免 agent 在 code block 裡示範 token 被誤觸發）、長度上限、單則訊息數量上限、以及『格式不對就留在可見文字裡讓 agent 自己發現』的一致慣例（docs/ab-token-protocol.md 明文記載此設計哲學）。\\\\n\\\\n2. Preamble 建構層（src/memory.ts，899行）：buildPreambleWithBreakdown() 是唯一 preamble builder，串接十幾個 note/block（sendFileNote, askNote, scheduleNote, skillUsedNote, goalDoneNote, restartNote, ...）；每個 note 對應一個 token 協定的『使用說明』文字段落，注入 preamble 讓三個 backend 在 session 建立時就讀到協定規則。\\\\n\\\\n3. Session 生命週期層（src/sessionManager.ts，1208行）：create() 是唯一呼叫 buildPreambleWithBreakdown 的地方，凍結進 session.memoryPreamble；get() 對存活 session 直接回傳既有物件不重建；四種情境才重建（client.isClosed / idle 逾時 sweepIdle / process crash exit handler / 使用者 /restart 或 /drop）。此凍結政策有專門 POLICIES 文件與 change-this-policy checklist 把關。\\\\n\\\\n4. Token 消費層（src/index.ts，1706行）：runPrompt 主流程與 specialist proxy 路徑（約1009行附近）都呼叫 transformReplyTokens 取得抽取結果，然後逐項處理（sendFiles → 送檔、stickerEmotions → 送貼圖、skillUsages → usageStore.markUsed 寫入 usage.json、goalDone/restartToken → 控制 goal loop 狀態機）。\\\\n\\\\n5. code、後者是純文字方法論規則（不在此 repo 的程式碼裡，是 global CLAUDE.md 的行為契約）。\\\\n\\\\n6. 部署層面（config.sessionCwd，src/config.ts:75）：整個 bridge 是綁定單一固定工作目錄的部署（一個 bridge process 對一個目標專案），非『依訊息切換多目標專案』架構；20+ 處程式碼（moa.ts/relay.ts/specialist.ts/sessionManager.ts 等）都直接引用同一個 config.sessionCwd。\\\\n\\\\n7. bridge 端目前沒有任何『執行外部指令覆核』的既有模式（全庫 grep child_process/spawn/execFile，命中的 27 個檔案裡沒有一個是『bridge 主動跑 tsc/lint 之類外部驗證指令』的封裝）——若初步構想的『bridge 端 npx tsc --noEmit 覆核』要落地，是全新能力，沒有既有 pattern The workflow's Explore phase has completed and produced structured output containing:

1. **existingArchitecture**: A detailed 7-point summary of the telegram-kiro-bridge architecture including:
   - Token protocol layer (observerTransformer.ts)
   - Preamble construction layer (memory.ts)
   - Session lifecycle layer (sessionManager.ts)
   - Token consumption layer (index.ts)
   - Goal/Turn circuit breaker (goalStore.ts)
   - Deployment layer (config.sessionCwd)
   - Lack of existing external command execution patterns

2. **constraints**: 7 major constraints that must be respected:
   - Preamble frozen-once-per-session policy
   - Cross-backend mechanism is text-based token protocol only
   - PreToolUse Hook is Claude-only, cross-CLI projection rejected
   - No existing child_process/spawn wrapper for external commands
   - Single fixed sessionCwd deployment (not multi-project)
   - Token payload length/count limits must follow existing conventions
   - Existing turn budget and R-5 circuit breakers are separate systems

3. **unknowns**: 7 areas needing clarification:
   - Six-dimension scoring logic validity not verified
   - Bridge-side tsc verification timing and concurrency handling
   - Whether Kiro/Codex will actually honor new self-eval token
   - How rework loop integrates with existing circuit breakers
   - L2 /dev-review from opt-in to mandatory implications
   - Preamble size budget for new instructions
   - Assumption that modifications are within sessionCwd

4. **factClaims**: 7 claims with evidence levels (A-grade and B-grade), documenting:
   - Preamble freeze policy with file/line references
   - Unified token extraction path
   - Token conventions for limits and deduplication
   - Single working directory architecture
   - Absence of external validation command wrappers
   - Two independent circuit breaker mechanisms operating in parallel
   - PreToolUse Hook's Claude-specific implementation preventing cross-CLI reuse

This structured discovery provides the foundation for moving into the Propose, Adversarial, and Synthesize phases.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Architecture Exploration Completed for Self-Evaluation Design</title>
<subtitle>Workflow Explore phase documented 7 constraints, 7 unknowns, and 7 evidence-graded fact claims about bridge architecture</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Explore phase identified 7 architectural layers: token protocol (observerTransformer.ts 761 lines), preamble construction (memory.ts 899 lines), session lifecycle (sessionManager.ts 1208 lines), token consumption (index.ts 1706 lines), goal/turn circuit breaker (goalStore.ts), deployment (config.sessionCwd), and absence of external command execution patterns</fact>
<fact>Seven hard constraints documented: preamble-frozen-once-per-session, text-based token protocol only cross-backend mechanism, PreToolUse Hook Claude-only with cross-CLI projection rejected, no existing child_process/spawn wrapper, single fixed sessionCwd deployment, token payload conventions must align with existing limits, separate turn budget and R-5 circuit breaker systems</fact>
<fact>Seven unknowns requiring design decisions: six-dimension scoring validity, bridge-side tsc timing and concurrency, Kiro/Codex compliance verification, rework loop integration with circuit breakers, L2 /dev-review opt-in to mandatory implications, preamble size budget, sessionCwd modification assumption</fact>
<fact>Seven fact claims with A-grade evidence (file:line references) and one B-grade claim (memory index reference without reading original file)</fact>
<fact>Key finding: bridge has no existing pattern for executing external validation commands (tsc/lint), making bridge-side tsc verification a completely new capability</fact>
<fact>Critical constraint: CLAUDE.md R-5 needs-attention circuit breaker operates at workflow review round level while goalStore.ts maxTurns operates at bridge session turn level - two separate systems that cannot share state</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow's Explore phase completed its architectural investigation and produced comprehensive structured output documenting the telegram-kiro-bridge system's current state. The exploration identified seven distinct architectural layers, from the 761-line observerTransformer.ts token extraction system through to the session lifecycle management. Seven hard constraints were documented with file:line evidence, most critically: (1) the preamble-frozen-snapshot policy enforced by sessionManager.create() calling buildPreambleWithBreakdown exactly once, (2) text-based token protocol as the only mechanism guaranteed to work across all three backends (Kiro CLI, Codex, Claude Agent), and (3) the absence of any existing child_process/spawn wrapper for external validation, meaning bridge-side tsc verification would be entirely new infrastructure. The exploration also surfaced seven unknowns requiring design phase resolution, including how the proposed rework loop would integrate with two existing but separate circuit breaker systems (goalStore.ts maxTurns for session turns vs CLAUDE.md R-5 for workflow rounds). All fact claims were graded by evidence level (A-grade with file:line citations, B-grade with memory index only), establishing a foundation for the subsequent Propose phase to generate competing design approaches.</narrative>

<concept>trade-off</concept>
</concepts>

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Token Protocol and Session Lifecycle Implementation Details Verified</title>
<subtitle>Concrete code evidence gathered for token limits, preamble injection latch, and goal turn budget system</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Token per-message limits confirmed in observerTransformer.ts: SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG=3, SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG=5, GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG=1, BALANCED_REASON_MAX=500 chars</fact>
<fact>Preamble injection is a one-time latch: sessionManager.ts calls buildPreambleWithBreakdown once at line 438, sets preambleInjected=true at line 571, never resets</fact>
<fact>Goal turn budget system uses DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS=5 defined at goalStore.ts:84, with markRestart() checking turns_used >= max_turns at line 462</fact>
<fact>Reason field truncation strategy: observerTransformer.ts lines 479/494/503 silently truncate over-length reasons to BALANCED_REASON_MAX instead of rejecting tokens</fact>
<fact>Session preamble injection differs by provider type: direct providers inject via system message (preambleInjected=true immediately), ACP providers inject via first user message (preambleInjected starts false, set true after first prompt)</fact>
<fact>Goal budget exhaustion check at goalStore.ts:365 uses willPause = nextTurns >= max_turns to determine when to pause continuation loop</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Following the architecture exploration phase, the workflow performed targeted grep operations to verify specific implementation details that will constrain the self-evaluation design. The verification confirmed precise token protocol limits established in observerTransformer.ts: up to 3 schedules, 5 skill usages, and 1 goal completion per message, with reason fields capped at 500 characters via silent truncation rather than rejection. The preamble injection mechanism was verified as a strict one-time operation - sessionManager.ts:438 calls buildPreambleWithBreakdown exactly once during session creation, and the preambleInjected flag at line 571 acts as a latch that never resets within a session's lifetime (only direct providers bypass this by using system messages). The goal turn budget system operates independently with DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS=5, where markRestart() at line 462 explicitly checks if turns_used has reached max_turns before allowing restart operations. These concrete implementation details provide the evidence base for the Propose phase to design a self-evaluation token that respects existing conventions (similar per-message limits and reason field sizes) while integrating with the separate goal turn budget and potential R-5 circuit breaker systems without conflation.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>gotcha</concept>
</concepts>

<file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
<file>src/goalStore.ts</file>
</files_read>
<files_modified>
</files_modified>
</observation>

自算自報），bridge 端不做裁判、只對「型別驗證」這一維度做事後抽查並標記不一致，不阻擋已送出的回覆。閾值/重做上限不新開第三套計數器，直接掛到既有 R-5 / goalStore 熔斷路徑上。誠實分級：L1（Kiro/Codex）=純文字自律+事後抽查，L2（Claude）=疊加既有 opt-in `/dev-review` 異源 skeptic 覆核，不假裝三個 backend 既有唯一呼叫點；🟢A級：sessionManager.ts create() 是唯一呼叫點（POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md 已載明），新增 note 不需要新的注入路徑，但會增加 preamble 字數預算，需納入既有 breakdown 統計物件\",\"src/observerTransformer.ts — 新增 SELF_EVAL_RE（或併入 Phase-1 balanced-scanner 清單），格式 <<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>>；比照 SKILL_USED_RE 的 charset/長度上限慣例（breakdown/evidence 各自套用 BALANCED_REASON_MAX=500 同量級或新常數）、SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG=1（比照 GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG=1，語意上一則回覆只代表一次自評，多次出現時先到先得、其餘留在可見文字裡不觸發）；沿用『格式不對就留在可見文字裡』的既有設計哲學，不 silently drop\",\"src/selfEvalStore.ts（新檔）— append-only 記錄每次 SELF_EVAL 結果（session/taskId/total/breakdown/timestamp），比照 usageStore.ts 的讀寫慣例（沿用同一種持久化 idiom，不另創第三種），供 rework 次數判斷與跨 session 追溯用\",\"src/index.ts（runPrompt 主流程 + specialist proxy 消費點，約1009行附近兩處）— 新增分支消費 transform() 回傳的 selfEval 欄位：total∈[60,79) 時注入下一輪 rework 提示（非重建 preamble，只是下一則訊息的補充文字）；同一 taskId 累計達第 3 次 rework 時改呼叫既有 goalStore markRestart/escalation 路徑而非新開計數器；total<60 直接走同一條既有熔斷路徑；🟡B級待驗證：markRestart 現有呼叫前置條件未經探索確認，實作前需 grep 其既有呼叫者核實\",\"src/tscSpotCheck.ts（新檔，全新能力）— 純 advisory、非阻塞：僅當 breakdown 宣稱『型別驗證』滿分且 config.sessionCwd 可解析時，在背景（不擋已送出的回覆）用 child_process 執行 npx tsc --noEmit（60s timeout）；單一 in-memory per-session boolean lock 防重入、5分鐘 cooldown 防重複觸發；不一致時額外送一則 Telegram 訊息標記 [self-eval-discrepancy]，不回滾、不重試；🟡B級：bridge 目前無任何執行外部指令的既有封裝（27個 grep 命中皆非此類 wrapper），此模組是設計中唯一真正新增的執行期能力，刻意縮小範圍（單維度、單鎖、無 queue）以壓低風險\",\"CLAUDE.md — 新增章節說明 SELF_EVAL 協定用法、閾值/重做規則、L1/L2 誠實分級標示，並明講 <60 與第3次 rework 沿用既有 R-5 熔斷、不新開計數器；此為方法論修改，依 R-2 需 cross-source review（/dev-review 異源 skeptic）而非自審通過\"],\"behaviorContracts\":[\"BC-1（token 抽取）：合法 <<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>>（total 為 0-100 數字、breakdown 六維度加總=total±0容差）被 observerTransformer.transform() 抽取為恰好一個 SelfEvalResult；同訊息重複出現時先到先得（比照既有 MAX_PER_MSG=1 token 慣例）；total 非數字或 breakdown 加總不等於 total 的格式錯誤 token，維持留在可見文字中不被抽取、不寫入 selfEvalStore（可用單元測試驗證：構造格式錯誤輸入 → transform() 回傳 selfEval=undefined 且原始字串仍出現在輸出文字）\",\"BC-2（重做上限掛既有熔斷）：total∈[60,79) 時，同一 taskId 最多連續注入 2 次 rework 提示；第 3 次改為呼叫既有 goalStore escalation 路徑而非產生第 3 次 rework 提示，且過程中不新增獨立計數器變數（可用單元測試驗證：模擬同 taskId 連續 3 次 total=70 的自評，斷言 rework 提示注入次數 ≤2 且第 3 次觸發的是既有 markRestart/escalation 呼叫）\",\"BC-3（tsc 抽查非阻塞且防重入）：tscSpotCheck 觸發時，使用者已收到的回覆訊息不受影響（送出時間不因抽查而延遲）；同一 session 在 cooldown 視窗（5分鐘）內第二次符合觸發條件時不重複執行 tsc、不重複送出 discrepancy 訊息（可用測試驗證：短時間內連續兩次型別驗證滿分宣稱，斷言 child_process 只被呼叫一次）\",\"BC-4（preamble 凍結相容）：selfEvalNote 僅在 buildPreambleWithBreakdown() 內、與既有其他 note 同一呼叫點注入，不新增任何呼叫 buildPreamble* 的新位置；即 grep sessionManager.ts 對 buildPreambleWithBreakdown 的呼叫次數在此改動前後仍為 1（驗證方式：對照 git diff，確認新增程式碼未新增第二個呼叫點，且該 note 的字數被計入既有 breakdown 回傳物件）\"],\"tradeoffs\":\"最大誠實限制：除了型別驗證這一單一維度有 tsc 抽查外，其餘五個維度（功能驗證/呼叫端影響/範圍紀律/完成度/回讀驗證）在 Kiro CLI / Codex 上完全無外部驗證，模型有灌水空間——本設計選擇明講而非假裝已解決。tscSpotCheck.ts 是全新的執行期能力（bridge 首次具備執行外部指令的封裝），雖刻意縮小到單維度+單鎖+無 queue，仍是本方案風險最集中的新增面；併發多 session 同時觸發時，因鎖是 per-session 而非全域，理論上仍可能多個 session 同時各跑一份 tsc（未做全域併發上限，MVP 階段接受此風險，非零成本）。重用 R-5/goalStore 熔斷而非新開計數器降低了複雜度，但代價是 <60 與第3次 rework 的使用者體驗會混入既有通用熔斷訊息，缺乏 self-eval 專屬說明，清晰度打折。preamble 因新增 selfEvalNote 而字數膨脹，且因凍結政策，若六維度權重事後被證明不合理，必須等下一次 /restart 才能生效，無法即時調整（此為既有政策的既定代價，非本設計新增）。markRestart 呼叫前置條件、tsc 抽查對非 config.sessionCwd 專案的意義，均為待驗證項，實作前需先補驗證而非直接假設可行。\",\"rippleImpact\":[\"src/memory.ts buildPreambleWithBreakdown() — 呼叫者窮舉：僅 sessionManager.ts create()（🟢A級，POLICIES 明文），改動風險低但需同步更新其回傳型別以納入 selfEvalNote 字數統計\",\"src/observerTransformer.ts transform() — 呼叫者窮舉：src/index.ts runPrompt 主流程 + specialist proxy 消費點（約1009行附近）共2處；新增 selfEval 欄位屬 additive 而非 breaking（TS 結構型別加欄位不影響既有解構），但兩處消費點都需要新增分支處理，若只改其中一處會造成 specialist proxy 路徑漏接自評結果——需逐一確認兩處都更新\",\"src/goalStore.ts markRestart/escalation — 新增呼叫來源（SELF_EVAL 熔斷路徑）：既有呼叫者/前置條件未在本次探索中確認（🟡B級待驗證），實作前必須 grep markRestart 既有呼叫點，核實是否有隱含狀態前提（例如預期特定欄位已設置）會被新呼叫來源違反\",\"src/selfEvalStore.ts — 全新檔案，零既有呼叫者，無 ripple，但需遵循 usageStore.ts 既有讀寫慣例（append-only vs mutation 選擇、是否需要檔案鎖）避免發明第三種持久化模式\",\"src/tscSpotCheck.ts — 全新檔案，bridge 首次引入 child_process 執行外部指令的封裝；🟡B級：全庫 grep spawn/execFile/child_process 命中27檔案均非此類統一 wrapper，此為設計中最高新穎度的元件，需額外評估與既有 process 生命週期（session close/idle sweep/crash handler）是否有交互（例如 session 在 tsc 執行中被 sweepIdle 清除時的競態）\",\"CLAUDE.md 新增章節 — 依 R-2（方法論修改需 cross-source review）不可用自審 finding 覆蓋跳過，需走 /dev-review 異源 skeptic 覆核後才可視為定案\",\"goalStore.ts 既有 R-5（方法論文字層 needs-attention 熔斷）與 DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS（bridge runtime 層熔斷）本屬不同管轄對象——本設計把 SELF_EVAL 的 <60/第3次rework 掛到 goalStore 的 runtime 熔斷路徑而非方法論層 R-5 計數器，需在 CLAUDE.md 新章節中明確澄清此掛接對象是 runtime 層而非文字方法論層，避免未來維護者混淆兩者\"]}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"\"Structured output provided successfully\""</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
This is the first design proposal from the Propose phase of the workflow. It's a "Reuse-first MVP" approach that focuses on minimal new infrastructure - adding a token, a preamble note, and a single advisory bridge-side spot-check for type validation only. The key points:

1. 100% of scoring logic stays in preamble text (agent self-calculates)
2. Bridge only does post-hoc spot-checking on "type validation" dimension using tsc
3. No new circuit breaker counter - hooks into existing R-5/goalStore paths
4. Honest tiering: L1 (Kiro/Codex) = text-based self-discipline + spot-check, L2 (Claude) = adds existing opt-in /dev-review
5. New files: selfEvalStore.ts, tscSpotCheck.ts
6. Four behavior contracts (BC-1 to BC-4) for verification
7. Major tradeoff: Only type validation has external verification, other 5 dimensions have no external check on Kiro/Codex
8. tscSpotCheck.ts is the highest novelty component - bridge's first child_process wrapper for external commands

This is structured output from an agent in the Propose phase, documenting one of three competing design approaches (MVP-first angle).

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>First Design Proposal: Reuse-First MVP Approach Generated</title>
<subtitle>MVP-first angle proposes minimal infrastructure with single-dimension bridge verification and existing circuit breaker reuse</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>MVP approach places 100% of scoring logic in preamble text with agent self-calculation, bridge only spot-checks type validation dimension via tsc</fact>
<fact>New token format <<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>> with MAX_PER_MSG=1, follows existing BALANCED_REASON_MAX=500 conventions</fact>
<fact>Five new/modified modules: src/memory.ts (selfEvalNote addition), src/observerTransformer.ts (SELF_EVAL_RE regex), src/selfEvalStore.ts (new append-only store), src/index.ts (consumption logic), src/tscSpotCheck.ts (new external command wrapper)</fact>
<fact>Four behavior contracts defined: BC-1 token extraction with format validation, BC-2 rework limit hooks existing goalStore escalation, BC-3 tsc spot-check non-blocking with 5-min cooldown, BC-4 preamble-frozen compatibility</fact>
<fact>Rework logic: 60-79 score triggers up to 2 rework prompts, 3rd attempt or <60 score escalates to existing goalStore circuit breaker</fact>
<fact>tscSpotCheck.ts is bridge's first child_process wrapper - single per-session boolean lock, 60s timeout, 5-min cooldown, advisory-only (doesn't block reply)</fact>
<fact>Major tradeoff acknowledged: only type validation has external verification, other 5 dimensions (functional verification, caller impact, scope discipline, completeness, read-back) have no external check on Kiro/Codex backends</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow's Propose phase generated its first design proposal from the MVP-first perspective, prioritizing minimal new infrastructure while reusing existing patterns. The core philosophy is to keep scoring logic entirely in preamble text (agent self-calculates and self-reports via new <<SELF_EVAL:total|breakdown|evidence>> token), with bridge providing only advisory spot-checking for the type validation dimension via a new tscSpotCheck.ts module. This marks the bridge's first introduction of child_process-based external command execution, deliberately scoped narrowly (single dimension, per-session lock, 5-minute cooldown) to minimize risk. The proposal reuses existing circuit breaker infrastructure rather than creating a third counter system - scores 60-79 get up to 2 rework attempts, then escalate to goalStore's existing markRestart/escalation path on the 3rd attempt or immediate escalation for scores below 60. Five modules would be touched: memory.ts adds selfEvalNote to preamble, observerTransformer.ts adds SELF_EVAL_RE extraction, new selfEvalStore.ts tracks evaluation history, index.ts adds consumption logic at two points (main runPrompt and specialist proxy), and new tscSpotCheck.ts provides non-blocking type checking. Four behavior contracts provide verification hooks - BC-1 for token extraction correctness, BC-2 for rework limit enforcement, BC-3 for non-blocking spot-check with anti-reentrancy, BC-4 for preamble-frozen policy compatibility. The major acknowledged tradeoff is honest about the limitation: only one of six dimensions gets external verification, leaving substantial trust in agent self-honesty for Kiro/Codex backends.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>trade-off</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
</concepts>

</observation>

content.thinking:
The workflow's Propose phase has now generated two additional design proposals:

1. **Second proposal (Robustness-first)**: A three-layer defense approach with:
   - Agent self-reports six dimensions
   - Bridge recalculates total from breakdown (doesn't trust reported total)
   - Optional type verification via tsc
   - Independent minimal counter separate from goalStore and R-5
   - New modules: selfEvalStore.ts, tscCheckQueue.ts (with queue, timeout, fail-open)
   - Honest labeling of unverified assumptions about backend compliance

2. **Third proposal (Simplicity-first)**: Radical simplification to:
   - Single score + one-line evidence only (no six-dimension breakdown)
   - No external process execution (no tsc verification)
   - No new retry/circuit breaker counters
   - Observability-only (not a quality gate) - low scores just flag for human follow-up
   - Minimal preamble addition (5-8 lines)
   - Same treatment across all three backends (no L1/L2 distinction)
   - Reasoning: "ceremony has zero correctness gains" (A-F reference), six dimensions vs one dimension equally unverifiable since both are self-eval

Now I have three competing designs from different perspectives (MVP-first, Robustness-first, Simplicity-first). The workflow should be moving into the Adversarial phase next where skeptics will critique each design.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Second and Third Design Proposals Generated: Robustness and Simplicity Approaches</title>
<subtitle>Robustness approach adds bridge-side recalculation defense layer; Simplicity approach eliminates six-dimension ceremony for single-score observability</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Robustness-first proposal (2nd) adds three-layer defense: agent self-report, bridge recalculates total from breakdown (never trusts reported total), optional tsc spot-check</fact>
<fact>Robustness approach creates independent minimal counter (selfEvalStore.ts) separate from both goalStore.maxTurns and CLAUDE.md R-5 to avoid conflation</fact>
<fact>Robustness adds tscCheckQueue.ts with concurrency limit 1, 30s timeout, fail-open design, and explicit logging of skipped checks</fact>
<fact>Simplicity-first proposal (3rd) eliminates six-dimension breakdown entirely, using only <<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>> with single 0-100 score and 500-char evidence field</fact>
<fact>Simplicity approach explicitly rejects external command execution (tsc verification), new circuit breaker counters, and L1/L2 tiering as unnecessary complexity</fact>
<fact>Simplicity positions self-eval as observability-only signal (not quality gate), with low scores triggering flag but no automatic rework loop - follow-up via existing Section 11.5/R-5 text-layer mechanisms</fact>
<fact>Simplicity rationale cites "A-F already proved ceremony has zero correctness gains" and argues six dimensions vs one dimension equally unverifiable since both rely on self-eval</fact>
<fact>Three proposals now complete from MVP-first (5 modules, bridge tsc spot-check, reuse existing circuit breakers), Robustness-first (independent counter, bridge recalculation, fail-open queue), and Simplicity-first (minimal token, zero execution, observability-only)</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow's Propose phase completed generation of three competing design approaches from distinct philosophical perspectives. The second (robustness-first) proposal takes a defensive stance: while keeping six-dimension scoring, it adds a critical trust layer - bridge-side recalculation of the total from breakdown values, explicitly never trusting the agent's reported total score. This creates a "verify but don't trust" model with the addition of tscCheckQueue.ts implementing a proper concurrent queue (limit 1) with timeout and fail-open semantics. Critically, this proposal creates selfEvalStore.ts as an independent minimal state machine explicitly separated from both goalStore.maxTurns (runtime layer) and CLAUDE.md R-5 (methodology layer) to avoid the conflation risk identified in exploration. The third (simplicity-first) proposal takes a radically different direction, arguing that six-dimension scoring is ceremonial complexity with no actual verification benefit since self-eval remains fundamentally unverifiable regardless of granularity. It strips down to <<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>> with a single 0-100 score, eliminates all external command execution (no tsc), removes new circuit breaker infrastructure, and explicitly positions the mechanism as observability-only rather than a quality gate. The simplicity proposal's core argument references "A-F" (likely a prior analysis) showing ceremony contributes zero correctness gains, therefore the complexity of six dimensions just adds cost without buying real verifiability. With three proposals now complete - MVP (reuse-first with minimal bridge verification), Robustness (defensive layering with independent infrastructure), and Simplicity (observability-only with minimal ceremony) - the workflow should proceed to the Adversarial phase where skeptics attempt to find fatal flaws in each approach.</narrative>

<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>problem-solution</concept>
</concepts>

  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"--- observerTransformer top ---\\n// Unified bridge-token extraction. Replaces the three separate\\n// extract*Tokens functions that index.ts defined inline. Covers:\\n//\\n//   <<SEND_FILE:abs-path>>      — existing\\n//   <<SCHEDULE:time|prompt>>    — existing\\n//   <<ASK:question_id|...>>     — existing\\n//   <<SKILL_USED:name|reason>>  — new (AB-1)\\n//   <<GOAL_DONE:reason>>        — new (AB-1)\\n//\\n// Token syntax, limits, and semantics are defined by\\n// `docs/ab-token-protocol.md`. This module is the one extraction path —\\n// no parallel implementations anywhere.\\n//\\n// Design notes:\\n//\\n//  • Code-region masking is mandatory for every regex. Agents frequently\\n//    paste token examples inside ``` fenced or `inline` code blocks to\\n//    explain the protocol; without masking those examples would be\\n//    treated as live tokens and accidentally trigger real side effects.\\n//    The mask/restore pair lives here and is private — callers don't\\n//    need to know about sentinels.\\n//\\n//  • ASK parsing keeps its full-featured parseAskInner function inline\\n//    here so SKILL_USED's simpler parser can sit next to it. The\\n//    Telegram-UI layer (buildAskKeyboard / askRegistry / replyWithAsk)\\n//    stays in index.ts — that's not transformation, it's rendering.\\n//\\n//  • SCHEDULE's time-parsing dependencies (parseDuration,\\n//    parseAbsoluteTime, parseRecurrence, computeNextRunAt) are injected\\n//    via the `scheduleParsers` parameter of `transform()` so this file\\n//    doesn't pull in the whole schedule-store module graph.\\n\\nimport type { Recurrence } from \\\"./recurrence.js\\\";\\nimport { STICKER_MAP } from \\\"./stickerMap.js\\\";\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Regexes — one per token. Each is anchored with `<<NAME:` prefix and\\n// terminates at `>>`. Payload body rejects `>` and CRLF so a stray `>`\\n// inside the payload terminates cleanly instead of eating downstream\\n// tokens.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport const SEND_FILE_RE = /<<SEND_FILE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_RE = /<<SCHEDULE:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const ASK_RE = /<<ASK:\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// 2026-05-02: reason bodies additionally reject `<` so an agent quoting\\n// other tokens inside a reason (e.g. <<GOAL_DONE:describes <<RESTART:foo>>>>)\\n// can't collapse the outer match onto the inner `>>`. SEND_FILE / SCHEDULE\\n// / ASK bodies keep `<` allowed because their payloads (paths / time specs\\n// / key=val) don't typically contain it, and banning `<` there would break\\n// legitimate payloads like URLs.\\nexport const SKILL_USED_RE =\\n  /<<SKILL_USED:\\\\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,60})\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_RE = /<<GOAL_DONE:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n// RESTART token (C3, 2026-05-02). `<<RESTART:reason>>` or bare\\n// `<<RESTART>>` asks the bridge to exit(1) so the supervisor respawns us.\\n// The actual guardrail (goal active? cooldown? cap?) lives in runPrompt;\\n// this regex only extracts the payload. Reason is 1-500 chars, rejects\\n// `<` / `>` / `|` / CRLF so malformed payloads (including agents quoting\\n// other tokens inside the reason) fall through as visible text instead\\n// of collapsing the match on a nested `>>`.\\nexport const RESTART_RE = /<<RESTART:\\\\s*([^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{1,500}?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_RE = /<<RESTART>>/g;\\nexport const RESTART_BARE_REASON = \\\"agent-requested-restart\\\";\\nexport const STICKER_RE = /<<STICKER:\\\\s*([a-z_]{1,20})\\\\s*>>/g;\\nexport const CONTINUE_RE = /<<CONTINUE>>/g;\\n\\n// ASK sub-regexes (kept here because ASK payload parsing lives here too).\\nconst ASK_ID_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,32}$/;\\nconst ASK_KEY_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,20}$/;\\nconst ASK_LABEL_MAX = 40;\\n// Soft cap: labels over this width are accepted but logged. The skill\\n// (ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol) mandates >20 -> numbered body list\\n// with short labels. We do not reject here because rejecting would\\n// leave the whole token verbatim in chat; a log line gives operators\\n// something to grep when a user reports crowded/unreadable buttons.\\nconst ASK_LABEL_SOFT = 20;\\n\\n// Token-class limits (match docs/ab-token-protocol.md).\\nexport const SCHEDULE_PROMPT_MAX = 1000;\\nexport const SCHEDULE_MAX_PER_MSG = 3;\\nexport const SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG = 5;\\nexport const GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// RESTART: only honour the first <<RESTART:...>> in one reply. Extra\\n// tokens stay in visible text as a signal to the agent (same idea as\\n// GOAL_DONE extra-token handling).\\nexport const RESTART_MAX_PER_MSG = 1;\\n// 2026-05-03: balanced-body reason cap raised from 200 → 500 and\\n// switched from hard-reject to silent truncate. Live-observed agent\\n// failure mode: when a goal takes 10+ turns to complete, the GOAL_DONE\\n// reason summarising \\\"why I think we're done\\\" routinely runs 300-600\\n// chinese chars. The old 200-char hard reject left the token verbatim\\n// in text, goalDone=null, and the bridge kept scheduling continuation\\n// turns forever (\\\"GOAL_DONE failed to fire\\\" bug fact, 2026-05-03).\\n// Truncate keeps the token functional while preserving a sane upper\\n// bound that fits comfortably in the \\\"✓ Goal achieved: <reason>\\\" line\\n// on Telegram. Same cap applied to SKILL_USED and RESTART for policy\\n// consistency.\\nexport const BALANCED_REASON_MAX = 500;\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Payload interfaces\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport interface AskOption {\\n  key: string;\\n  label: string;\\n  recommended: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface AskEntry {\\n  questionId: string;\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
This is a critical development in the workflow - the Adversarial phase has completed with judge panel rankings and recommendations. Let me parse what happened:

1. **Fatal Flaws Identified** across all three proposals:
   - Flaws #1 & #2 (common to MVP and Robustness): tsc verification can be bypassed by agents, and the trigger condition contradicts known constraints (no way to detect .ts/.tsx changes on Kiro/Codex backends)
   - Flaw #3 (common to MVP and Robustness): Unsafe integration with existing circuit breakers - markRestart has unverified preconditions, evalScope binding undefined
   - Flaw #4 (all three): No evidence that Kiro/Codex will actually honor preamble self-eval instructions
   - Flaw #5 (all three): Treating unverified implementation details as behavior contracts, violating project's own Section 11 fact-checking discipline
   - Flaw #6 (MVP and Robustness): Nested/structured breakdown payload breaks existing flat-field convention

2. **Rankings**:
   - Simplicity-first: 7/10 - "only one without structurally fatal flaws"
   - MVP-first: 4/10 - "tsc spot-check can be gamed by agents"
   - Robustness-first: 3/10 - "biggest gap between packaging and actual effectiveness"

3. **Recommendation**: Base on Simplicity proposal with specific fixes and selective grafting from other proposals

4. **Verification Activity**: The bash command checked actual caller counts and usageStore mechanism to verify claims made in the proposals

This is a major milestone - the adversarial verification found that the two more complex proposals (MVP and Robustness) both have "structurally fatal flaws" where their core verification mechanisms can be bypassed by the agents being evaluated. The Simplicity approach won despite appearing less rigorous because it's honest about its limitations rather than creating false security theater.
The judges identified a fundamental problem: adding ceremonial verification steps doesn't improve correctness—it just creates exploitable gaps. The six-dimension scoring framework with bridge verification actually introduces more vulnerabilities than it prevents, giving false confidence while remaining gameable against the constraints we know are real.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>decision</type>
<title>Adversarial Review Rejected Complex Self-Evaluation Designs for Honest Observability</title>
<subtitle>Judge panel ranked Simplicity-first (7/10) over MVP and Robustness approaches due to structural fatal flaws in verification mechanisms</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Six fatal flaws identified across three proposals: tsc verification can be bypassed by agents reporting low type scores, trigger conditions contradict known constraints for Kiro/Codex backends, circuit breaker integrations have unverified preconditions, no evidence backends honor self-eval instructions, unverified implementation details treated as behavior contracts, nested payload breaks flat-field conventions</fact>
<fact>Simplicity-first proposal scored 7/10 as only design without structurally fatal verification bypass - acknowledges single 0-100 score with evidence field is observability-only, no external verification or automatic rework</fact>
<fact>MVP-first proposal scored 4/10 due to gameable tsc spot-check (agents can bypass by not reporting full type score) and unresolved conflict with Section 1 opt-in workflow policy</fact>
<fact>Robustness-first proposal scored 3/10 as "biggest gap between packaging and effectiveness" - bridge recalculation only sums agent-provided subscores (mathematically equivalent to agent summing), evalScope key undefined allowing counter reset by changing strings</fact>
<fact>Critical insight cited: "A-F already proved ceremony has zero correctness gains" - six-dimension scoring adds complexity without buying actual verifiability since self-eval remains fundamentally unverifiable</fact>
<fact>Verification bash command confirmed buildPreambleWithBreakdown has 2 callers (sessionManager.ts:438, scheduler.ts:291), transformReplyTokens has 6 call sites, usageStore.ts uses atomicJson.updateJson() for serialized read-modify-write concurrency</fact>
<fact>Recommendation: base on Simplicity proposal with fixes (evidence field use balanced-scanner not regex, verify usageStore append-only assumption, precise 0-100 validation, silent duplicate discard) plus lightweight grafting (evidence must cite file:line or action basis, preamble mentions check dimensions as thinking guide not machine-parsed fields)</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow's Adversarial phase completed with a decisive verdict rejecting the two more complex self-evaluation designs in favor of the minimalist observability-only approach. The judge panel identified six categories of fatal flaws, with the most damning being that both MVP-first and Robustness-first proposals contain verification mechanisms that can be trivially bypassed by the agents being evaluated. The MVP proposal's tsc spot-check only triggers when the type dimension reports full score - agents can game this by intentionally lowering type scores while inflating others. The Robustness proposal's trigger condition requires detecting .ts/.tsx file changes, but the exploration phase explicitly documented that PreToolUse hooks don't work on Kiro/Codex backends and cross-CLI projection was rejected, creating a direct contradiction. The judge panel's ranking placed Simplicity-first at 7/10 as "the only design without a structurally fatal flaw where the core mechanism fails under adversarial conditions." The key philosophical insight driving this decision references "A-F" (likely a prior analysis) showing ceremony contributes zero correctness gains - therefore six-dimension scoring with bridge verification creates false confidence theater rather than actual quality gates. The recommendation is to base implementation on the Simplicity proposal (single score with evidence, no external verification, observability-only positioning) while grafting specific improvements: use balanced-scanner for evidence field to handle nested token literals, verify usageStore's append-only assumption before writing behavior contracts, add preamble guidance mentioning check dimensions as thinking prompts rather than machine-parsed structure. The bash verification confirmed actual caller counts and concurrency mechanisms to support the decision. This represents a major architectural decision: choosing honest limitation acknowledgment over false security theater.</narrative>

<concept>gotcha</concept>
<concept>why-it-exists</concept>
</concepts>

At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. Tokens inside\\n * code regions are ignored. Malformed tokens are left in the visible\\n * text as documentation for the agent. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\\n  // Collect payloads in a mutable object while we do the .replace passes.\\n  const sendFiles: string[] = [];\\n  const scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[] = [];\\n  const askTokens: AskEntry[] = [];\\n  const skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[] = [];\\n  const seenSkillNames = new Set<string>();\\n  const seenAskIds = new Set<string>();\\n  let goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null = null;\\n  let restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null = null;\\n  let skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null = null;\\n  let wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null = null;\\n  let specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null = null;\\n  let moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null = null;\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 1 — balanced-scanner tokens (free-form reason bodies).\\n  // These run FIRST so that when an agent nests another token inside\\n  // a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:... <<RESTART:foo>> ...>>`), the outer\\n  // match consumes the whole span including the nested literal. Later\\n  // phases never see the inner token and can't trigger it by accident.\\n  //\\n  // Single forward pass over `masked`: at each `<<PREFIX:` (for any of\\n  // the three balanced-body token classes), we walk to the matching\\n  // top-level `>>` and hand the inner text to the right handler.\\n  // Crucially, the walk happens before any narrower extractor runs —\\n  // nested `<<...>>` inside a reason is consumed as part of the outer\\n  // span and never appears as an independent token.\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n  let stripped = runBalancedPhase(masked, {\\n    \\\"<<SKILL_USED:\\\": (inner) => {\\n      if (skillUsages.length >= SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG) return null;\\n      const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n      if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n      const name = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\",\"numLines\":175,\"startLine\":300,\"totalLines\":762}}"</outcome>

The workflow is now reading memory.ts to understand the buildPreambleWithBreakdown structure more deeply. This is likely preparing to write the final design specification that will detail how to add a new "selfEvalNote" to this preamble construction pipeline.

The code shows:
1. buildPreambleWithBreakdown returns a PreambleBreakdown object with character counts for each section
2. It calls buildPreambleSections which returns individual note/block sections
3. The text concatenation happens in a specific order (13 notes + rtkNote + shellSafetyNote + 7 blocks)
4. The toolNotes measurement adds up specific note lengths
5. Each block/note length is tracked separately in the return object

This is contextual reading to inform the Synthesize phase about how to properly integrate a new selfEvalNote into the existing preamble architecture.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Preamble Construction Architecture Deep-Dive for Integration Planning</title>
<subtitle>buildPreambleWithBreakdown concatenates 13 tool notes plus 7 contextual blocks with per-section character measurement for token budget tracking</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>buildPreambleWithBreakdown assembles preamble from buildPreambleSections output containing 13 tool notes (sendFile, ask, schedule, skillUsed, goalDone, restart, sticker, wikiQuery, skillPropose, continue, relayDelegate, specialistProxy, parallelDelegate) plus rtkNote and shellSafetyNote</fact>
<fact>Seven contextual blocks follow notes: envBlock, userProfileBlock, memoryBlock, dailylogBlock, wikiBlock, indexBlock, scriptsBlock in that specific concatenation order</fact>
<fact>PreambleBreakdown return type tracks total character length plus individual measurements: toolNotes (sum of 12 specific notes), envBlock, memoryBlock, dailylogBlock, wikiBlock, indexBlock, scriptsBlock, userProfileBlock, tailFacts Set</fact>
<fact>toolNotes measurement excludes parallelDelegateNote, rtkNote, and shellSafetyNote from its sum despite being in text concatenation</fact>
<fact>buildLocalPreamble provides minimal ~2000 token preamble variant for Ollama backend with only toolGuide and recent 5 facts</fact>
<fact>tailFacts Set construction strips markdown list bullets and fact IDs for deduplication in memory-recall system</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow performed a detailed examination of memory.ts's preamble construction architecture to understand exactly how a new selfEvalNote would integrate into the existing system. The code reveals a two-tier structure: buildPreambleSections generates individual text strings for each note and block, then buildPreambleWithBreakdown concatenates them in a fixed order while measuring each section's character contribution. The concatenation order is deliberate - all tool protocol notes come first (13 different token instructions), followed by runtime/safety notes (rtkNote, shellSafetyNote), then contextual information blocks (environment, user profile, memory, daily log, wiki, indexes, scripts). The PreambleBreakdown return object provides granular token budget visibility with 9 separate measurements, though interestingly the toolNotes sum only includes 12 of the notes despite 15+ being concatenated. This measurement asymmetry suggests toolNotes specifically tracks "token protocol instruction overhead" while other notes fall into different conceptual categories. For the Simplicity-based self-eval design, this architecture means adding selfEvalNote would require: (1) adding it to buildPreambleSections return signature, (2) inserting it into the concatenation sequence (likely after continueNote given semantic similarity as behavioral instruction), (3) deciding whether to include it in toolNotes measurement or create a separate tracking field, and (4) ensuring the note text stays minimal per the Simplicity philosophy to avoid preamble inflation.</narrative>

At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. Tokens inside\\n * code regions are ignored. Malformed tokens are left in the visible\\n * text as documentation for the agent. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\\n  // Collect payloads in a mutable object while we do the .replace passes.\\n  const sendFiles: string[] = [];\\n  const scheduleTokens: ScheduleTokenEntry[] = [];\\n  const askTokens: AskEntry[] = [];\\n  const skillUsages: SkillUsageEntry[] = [];\\n  const seenSkillNames = new Set<string>();\\n  const seenAskIds = new Set<string>();\\n  let goalDone: GoalDoneEntry | null = null;\\n  let restartToken: RestartTokenEntry | null = null;\\n  let skillPropose: SkillProposeEntry | null = null;\\n  let wikiQuery: WikiQueryEntry | null = null;\\n  let specialistProxy: SpecialistProxyEntry | null = null;\\n  let moaPlan: MoaPlanEntry | null = null;\\n\\n  // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n  // Phase 1 — balanced-scanner tokens (free-form reason bodies).\\n  // These run FIRST so that when an agent nests another token inside\\n  // a reason (e.g. `<<GOAL_DONE:... <<RESTART:foo>> ...>>`), the outer\\n  // match consumes the whole span including the nested literal. The key points I should capture:
1. The two-phase token extraction architecture - Phase 1 (balanced-scanner for free-form reason bodies) and Phase 2 (regex for structured bodies)
2. The balanced scanner's outer-first strategy that handles nested token literals
3. Handler semantics: returns string to replace, returns null to leave verbatim

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Two-Phase Token Extraction Architecture Details for Self-Eval Integration</title>
<subtitle>Phase 1 balanced-scanner handles nested literals via depth tracking, Phase 2 uses regex for structured payloads with different semantic guarantees</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Phase 1 balanced-scanner processes free-form reason body tokens (SKILL_USED, GOAL_DONE, RESTART, SKILL_PROPOSE, WIKI_QUERY, SPECIALIST_PROXY, MOA_PLAN) before Phase 2 regex tokens to prevent nested token literals from triggering</fact>
<fact>runBalancedPhase uses outer-first strategy with depth counter tracking << and >> pairs - outer token consumes entire span atomically including any nested <<...>> literals inside reason text</fact>
<fact>Handler return semantics: string replaces full <<PREFIX:...>> span, null leaves token verbatim and scanner continues past it for malformed tokens</fact>
<fact>Tokens must be single-line - newline (0x0a or 0x0d) inside candidate span aborts match and leaves opener visible as documentation for agent</fact>
<fact>Phase 2 regex-based tokens (SEND_FILE, SCHEDULE, ASK, STICKER, bare RESTART, CONTINUE) handle structured bodies where path/time-spec/key=value format prevents nested token issues</fact>
<fact>Scanner is O(n) single forward pass, code-region masking replaces ```...``` and `...` with sentinel \u0000CODE<n>\u0000 before extraction to prevent examples from triggering</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The workflow examined observerTransformer.ts's two-phase extraction architecture to understand where and how a new SELF_EVAL token would integrate. The architecture divides tokens into two semantic categories processed in strict order: Phase 1 handles "balanced-body" tokens with free-form reason text that might contain examples of other tokens (processed via runBalancedPhase with depth-tracking), while Phase 2 handles "structured-body" tokens with fixed formats like paths or key=value pairs (processed via simple regex replacement). The critical design insight is Phase 1's outer-first strategy - when scanning encounters <<RESTART:describes <<GOAL_DONE:foo>> here>>, the balanced scanner finds RESTART's opener first, walks to its balanced closer tracking << and >> depth, and consumes the entire span atomically. The inner <<GOAL_DONE:foo>> literal never gets extracted as a real GOAL_DONE because Phase 1 already removed it as part of the RESTART reason before Phase 2 regex patterns run. Handler functions return either a replacement string (token was valid, strip it) or null (token was malformed, leave it visible so agent sees its own error). For the Simplicity-based self-eval design with <<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>>, this architecture presents a choice: if evidence field can contain arbitrary text that might include token examples, it should use Phase 1 balanced-scanner; if evidence is constrained enough to never contain <<, it can use simpler Phase 2 regex. The Adversarial phase recommendation to "use balanced-scanner for evidence field" reflects understanding that agents explaining their evaluation might quote other tokens and need the nesting protection.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>gotcha</concept>
</concepts>

</files_modified>
</observation>
